```text
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## What this is

A simplified PTT-style BBS (批踢踢 clone) served over SSH. Built with
`charmbracelet/wish` (SSH framework) + `bubbletea` (TUI), persisted in
SQLite via the pure-Go `modernc.org/sqlite` driver. **Vim-style navigation
is a deliberate product decision** — `h`/`j`/`k`/`l` work alongside arrow
keys and Esc/Enter on every list/menu screen. Form screens (register,
post compose, water-balloon compose) intentionally do *not* bind h/l so
they remain usable as text-edit keys.

For the why-we-do-it-this-way notes mapping back to pttbbs internals, see
`docs/ptt_trace_code/`. For the testing strategy, see `docs/testing.md`.

## Common commands

```bash
make hostkey            # generate .ssh/host_ed25519 (run once before first run)
make run                # boots :2222, creates data/bbs.db with seed boards
make build              # produces ./sshbbs
make test               # plain go test
make test-race          # what we treat as the CI standard
make cover              # per-package coverage
make db-reset           # rm data/bbs.db*

# focused testing
go test -run TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity -race -v ./internal/store/...
go test -race ./internal/chat/...

# manual two-user smoke
ssh new@localhost -p 2222     # password ignored — fills the in-TUI register form
ssh alice@localhost -p 2222   # use the password set during register
```

If `make hostkey` is re-run, clients see `Host key verification failed`.
Fix: `ssh-keygen -R '[localhost]:2222'`.

## Architecture — the non-obvious parts

### SSH-user-as-login, with `new` as the register sentinel
`auth.ReservedUsernameNew = "new"`. The password-auth callback in
`internal/server/auth_middleware.go` accepts any password for SSH user
`new` and stashes a "register mode" flag in `ssh.Context`; the TUI
handler reads it and routes straight to the register screen. For any
other SSH username, `auth.VerifyLogin` runs bcrypt against the DB and
stashes the `user_id` (int64) in the context for `makeProgramHandler`
to load.

This means `auth.Register` rejects the literal username `new` — that
constant is referenced in two places (auth + middleware) and they must
not drift. `auth.go` defines it once.

### Wish uses `MiddlewareWithProgramHandler`, not `Middleware`
We pick the *ProgramHandler* variant deliberately
(`internal/server/server.go`) because we need the `*tea.Program` pointer
to register the session with `chat.Broker` and to `program.Send(tea.Quit())`
during graceful shutdown. The simpler `Middleware(handler)` form constructs
the program internally and hides the pointer — you can't broadcast
into it. Don't switch back.

### The chat broker takes a `Sender` interface, not `*tea.Program`
`chat.Sender` is `interface { Send(tea.Msg) }`. `*tea.Program` satisfies
it naturally. The interface exists purely so unit tests can substitute
a recording fake (`fakeSender` in `broker_test.go`) without spinning up
a real bubbletea program. If you find yourself wanting to mock the
broker, you probably want to add a method here, not refactor.

### Live broadcasts are "send to everyone, filter on receive"
For 推文 (push) updates, `screen_article_view.updatePushInput` calls
`broker.SendToAll(senderUID, PushAddedMsg{ArticleID, ...})`. Every
connected session receives the message; each article-view model
ignores it unless `msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID`. This is simpler than
tracking who's viewing what and good enough for our scale. When the
filter matches, we **re-fetch from DB** rather than trust the broker
payload — that way timestamps and `recommend_score` are always canonical.

Water balloons (`broker.Send(toUID, ...)`) target a single user; the
multi-session "雙開" case fans out to every live `Session` of that user.

### `Store.writeMu` is process-level, not row-level
`internal/store/store.go` exposes a single `sync.Mutex` that's grabbed
inside every multi-statement repo method (e.g. `PushRepo.Create` runs
`INSERT INTO pushes` and `UPDATE articles SET recommend_score` in one
transaction under the mutex). This keeps the cached score atomic from
the application's perspective and avoids `SQLITE_BUSY` flapping with
the pure-Go driver. Don't use `sql.Tx` ad-hoc inside repo methods
without also taking `writeMu`. The `pushes` test
(`TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`, 50 concurrent inserts) is the
canary — if it ever fails under `-race`, the mutex discipline broke.

### Migrations live under the store package, not the repo root
`internal/store/migrations/*.sql` is embedded with `go:embed
migrations/*.sql` from `internal/store/migrate.go`. `go:embed` can't
ascend with `..`, so the SQL files live next to the Go code that loads
them. Filename format `NNNN_name.sql`; `migrate.apply()` parses the
prefix as the version and tracks applied versions in the
`schema_migrations` table.
```
</details></tool-use>

## Structured Feature Mapping: Article Edit Permission System

Based on my codebase exploration, here's the complete map for implementing admin/mod article-edit capability:

---

### **A. Permission System**

**Role Definition & Rank:**
- **File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/role.go` (lines 3–38)
- **Four roles** (constant definitions, lines 9–14):
  ```go
  RoleGuest Role = "guest"  // read-only spectator
  RoleUser  Role = "user"   // registered, default
  RoleMod   Role = "mod"    // can delete anyone's article/push
  RoleAdmin Role = "admin"  // can promote/demote others
  ```
- **Rank ordering** (map, lines 16–21): Guest(0) < User(1) < Mod(2) < Admin(3)
- **Permission check method** (`Role.AtLeast()`, lines 25–32):
  ```go
  func (r Role) AtLeast(min Role) bool {
      rr, rok := roleRank[r]
      mr, mok := roleRank[min]
      if !rok || !mok {
          return false
      }
      return rr >= mr
  }
  ```

**User Schema & Session Integration:**
- **User struct**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/users.go` (lines 10–24)
  - Column: `Role` field (type `store.Role`, a TEXT column with CHECK constraint)
  - Loaded into session via `Deps.User` (pointer to `*store.User`; see `internal/tui/root.go` line 19)
- **How role loads**: `auth.VerifyLogin` → `UserRepo.GetByUserID()` → populates `Deps.User.Role`

**Recent commit intro (a5bd3bf):**
- Migration 0004 added `users.role` (CHECK enum) and `must_change_password`
- Seeded `admin` account with default `'admin'/'admin'` (overridable via `-admin-password` flag)
- Introduced `MustChangePasswordRemoteBlocked()` to block remote admin login until password rotated
- Deployed read-only enforcement: guests blocked from write screens via `Root.guestWriteBlocked()`

---

### **B. Article Authorship & Ownership**

**Article Schema:**
- **File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/articles.go` (lines 10–20)
- **Relevant fields**:
  ```go
  type Article struct {
      ID           int64
      BoardID      int64
      AuthorID     int64      // user.id
      AuthorUserID string     // user.user_id (display name)
      Title        string
      Body         string
      // ... RecommendScore, Filemode, CreatedAt
  }

func (m articleViewModel) canDeleteArticle() bool {
    if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
        return false
    }
    if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
        return true  // author can delete own article
    }

func (m articleViewModel) canDeletePush(idx int) bool {
    // mirrors canDeleteArticle for pushes
    if m.deps.User == nil || idx < 0 || idx >= len(m.pushes) {
        return false
    }
    if m.deps.User.ID == m.pushes[idx].UserID {
        return true
    }
    return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
}

func (m articleViewModel) canPush() bool {
    return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest
}

# Plan: Markdown round-trip for articles + Welcome seeding + edit/export

## A. Markdown round-trip format

Recommended format — **YAML-ish frontmatter with a hand-rolled parser**, no new dep. The frontmatter only needs string and int scalars; we don't need YAML's full grammar. Adding `gopkg.in/yaml.v3` for ~6 fields is overkill and the user prefers minimal complexity.

```
---
title: 歡迎來到 SSH-BBS
board: Welcome
author: admin
created_at: 2026-04-30T12:34:56Z
score: 0
---

(article body here, arbitrary Markdown / plain text, possibly
including a literal `---` line which must NOT be misread as
frontmatter — see "edge cases" below.)

<!-- pushes -->
- 推 alice  2026-04-30T12:35:01Z  great post
- 噓 bob    2026-04-30T12:35:14Z  disagree
- → carol   2026-04-30T12:35:20Z  fyi
```

Rules that keep the parser tiny:
- Frontmatter delimiter is exactly `---\n` at byte 0; closing delimiter is `\n---\n`. If byte 0 is not `---`, there is no frontmatter (treat the whole file as body, infer minimal metadata).
- Each frontmatter line is `key: value` (split on first `:`), trim spaces. Unknown keys are preserved into a `map[string]string` for forward-compat but ignored by `Parse`.
- Pushes block is delimited by an HTML comment `<!-- pushes -->` on its own line, then one push per `- ` line. Format: `<glyph> <author> <RFC3339-timestamp>  <body>`. Glyph maps `推→push`, `噓→boo`, `→→arrow`. Body may contain spaces but NOT newlines (multi-line push bodies aren't supported by the DB schema — see edge cases).
- Timestamps are RFC3339 in UTC. Empty fields become zero values.

Why hand-rolled wins:
- Zero new dependency, zero `go.sum` churn.
- Parser fits in ~80 LOC plus tests.
- Keeps "every article expressible in Markdown" honest: anyone can `cat` the file and read it; round-trip is `Format(Parse(s))`.
- Trade-off: we can't represent escape sequences (e.g. a title that literally contains `\n`). Fine for our domain — titles are `CharLimit=64` single-line.

## B. `internal/markdown/` package

**Files to create**:
- `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/markdown/markdown.go` — pure functions, no DB
- `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/markdown/markdown_test.go`

**Signatures**:
```go
package markdown

type FormatOpts struct {
    IncludePushes bool
    BoardName     string // resolved by caller from BoardID; pure pkg has no DB
}

// Format renders an article (+ optional pushes) as the format described in (A).
func Format(a *store.Article, pushes []*store.Push, opts FormatOpts) (string, error)

// Parsed is the round-trip target. We deliberately do NOT return *store.Article
// because Parse runs without DB context — Article.BoardID/AuthorID need lookup.
// Caller resolves names → IDs before INSERT.
type Parsed struct {
    Title       string
    BoardName   string
    AuthorName  string
    CreatedAt   time.Time // zero if absent
    Body        string
    Pushes      []ParsedPush
    Extra       map[string]string // unknown frontmatter keys, preserved
}
type ParsedPush struct {
    Kind      store.PushKind
    Author    string
    CreatedAt time.Time
    Body      string
}

func Parse(md string) (*Parsed, error)
```

Importing `internal/store` for `Article`/`Push`/`PushKind` is fine — `markdown` is a leaf package, nothing in `store` imports it. (Avoids reinventing types.)

**Tests** (`markdown_test.go`):
- `TestFormat_RoundTrip` — make Article+pushes, Format, Parse, assert field equality (modulo time.Time monotonic stripping).
- `TestParse_NoFrontmatter` — body-only file → Title="", Body=whole file.
- `TestParse_BodyContainingTripleDash` — body that legitimately has `---` lines mid-paragraph must not terminate frontmatter parsing (we already stop at the FIRST `\n---\n` after the opener).
- `TestParse_PushesOnly` — frontmatter + empty body + pushes block.
- `TestParse_UnknownFrontmatterKey` — preserved into `Extra`.
- `TestFormat_OmitPushesWhenIncludePushesFalse`.

## C. Seed-from-embedded-markdown

**Files to create**:
- `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/seed_articles/welcome.md` — the actual content; user maintains in their editor.
- `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/seed_articles.go` — embeds + applies.

**Signature**:
```go
//go:embed seed_articles/*.md
var seedArticlesFS embed.FS

// SeedFromEmbedded inserts each *.md file under seed_articles/ as an article
// in the named board, attributed to authorUserID. It is a no-op for any board
// that already contains at least one article — that way edits made via the
// in-TUI edit screen survive restarts.
//
// markdownPkg is passed in (as a function value or type) to avoid an import
// cycle: store cannot import markdown if markdown imports store.
func (r *ArticleRepo) SeedFromEmbedded(ctx context.Context, board, authorUserID string,
    parse func(string) (title, body string, err error)) error
```

Actually cleaner: keep `markdown.Parse` out of `store` entirely. Put the seeder where it can import both — under a new tiny package or in `cmd/sshbbs`. Recommended: put the seeder at `internal/seed/articles.go`, importing both `store` and `markdown`:

```go
// internal/seed/articles.go
package seed

//go:embed articles/*.md
var fs embed.FS

// Articles seeds embedded markdown into the named boards. It looks up the
// admin user by reserved username, the board by name, and INSERTs only if
// the board has zero articles. Idempotent.
func Articles(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, adminUserID string) error
```

Wire into `cmd/sshbbs/main.go` after `auth.SeedSystemAccounts`:
```go
if err := seed.Articles(ctx, st, auth.ReservedUsernameAdmin); err != nil {
    log.Fatalf("seed articles: %v", err)
}
```

The seed file naming convention I recommend: `internal/seed/articles/<board-name>__<slug>.md` (board stem followed by `__`, then a slug). The seeder splits the filename on `__` to know which board to target — cleaner than reading frontmatter twice. Alternative: trust the `board:` frontmatter key and skip the filename convention. Either works; I'd pick filename for "you can grep `Welcome__` and see what gets seeded into Welcome".

**Edge case — idempotency**: check `len(ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, 1)) == 0` before insert. Don't use a "seeded once" marker table — that conflicts with the user-stated requirement that admin edits later must NOT be clobbered. If the operator wants to re-seed after deleting all articles, deletion is rare and they can do it.

**Tests** (`internal/seed/articles_test.go`):
- `TestArticles_SeedsWhenBoardEmpty` — fresh store, run seed, assert Welcome has exactly the embedded count.
- `TestArticles_NoOpWhenBoardHasContent` — pre-insert one article, run seed, assert article count unchanged.
- `TestArticles_MissingBoardLogsAndContinues` — file targets a board name that doesn't exist; seeder logs+skips, doesn't fail startup.

**Edge cases**:
- A user editing while another user pushes — both writes go through `writeMu` so they serialize. The push's `recommend_score` increment + the edit's title/body update don't conflict (different columns).
- Concurrent edits by author + mod — last writer wins (no row-level optimistic concurrency). Acceptable for MVP; flag as TODO if user cares.
- `updated_at` ordering — `ListByBoard` still orders by `created_at DESC, id DESC`. Don't change that or the bookmark invariant breaks.

- Don't put the seed file content under version-controlled `data/` (which is `.gitignore`d) — it must live alongside Go source so `go:embed` picks it up.

1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## What this is
6	
7	A simplified PTT-style BBS (批踢踢 clone) served over SSH. Built with
8	`charmbracelet/wish` (SSH framework) + `bubbletea` (TUI), persisted in
9	SQLite via the pure-Go `modernc.org/sqlite` driver. **Vim-style navigation
10	is a deliberate product decision** — `h`/`j`/`k`/`l` work alongside arrow
11	keys and Esc/Enter on every list/menu screen. Form screens (register,
12	post compose, water-balloon compose) intentionally do *not* bind h/l so
13	they remain usable as text-edit keys.
14	
15	For the why-we-do-it-this-way notes mapping back to pttbbs internals, see
16	`docs/ptt_trace_code/`. For the testing strategy, see `docs/testing.md`.
17	
18	## Common commands
19	
20	```bash
21	make hostkey            # generate .ssh/host_ed25519 (run once before first run)
22	make run                # boots :2222, creates data/bbs.db with seed boards
23	make build              # produces ./sshbbs
24	make test               # plain go test
25	make test-race          # what we treat as the CI standard
26	make cover              # per-package coverage
27	make db-reset           # rm data/bbs.db*
28	
29	# focused testing
30	go test -run TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity -race -v ./internal/store/...
31	go test -race ./internal/chat/...
32	
33	# manual two-user smoke
34	ssh new@localhost -p 2222     # password ignored — fills the in-TUI register form
35	ssh alice@localhost -p 2222   # use the password set during register
36	```
37	
38	If `make hostkey` is re-run, clients see `Host key verification failed`.
39	Fix: `ssh-keygen -R '[localhost]:2222'`.
40	
41	## Architecture — the non-obvious parts
42	
43	### SSH-user-as-login, with `new` as the register sentinel
44	`auth.ReservedUsernameNew = "new"`. The password-auth callback in
45	`internal/server/auth_middleware.go` accepts any password for SSH user
46	`new` and stashes a "register mode" flag in `ssh.Context`; the TUI
47	handler reads it and routes straight to the register screen. For any
48	other SSH username, `auth.VerifyLogin` runs bcrypt against the DB and
49	stashes the `user_id` (int64) in the context for `makeProgramHandler`
50	to load.
51	
52	This means `auth.Register` rejects the literal username `new` — that
53	constant is referenced in two places (auth + middleware) and they must
54	not drift. `auth.go` defines it once.
55	
56	### Wish uses `MiddlewareWithProgramHandler`, not `Middleware`
57	We pick the *ProgramHandler* variant deliberately
58	(`internal/server/server.go`) because we need the `*tea.Program` pointer
59	to register the session with `chat.Broker` and to `program.Send(tea.Quit())`
60	during graceful shutdown. The simpler `Middleware(handler)` form constructs
61	the program internally and hides the pointer — you can't broadcast
62	into it. Don't switch back.
63	
64	### The chat broker takes a `Sender` interface, not `*tea.Program`
65	`chat.Sender` is `interface { Send(tea.Msg) }`. `*tea.Program` satisfies
66	it naturally. The interface exists purely so unit tests can substitute
67	a recording fake (`fakeSender` in `broker_test.go`) without spinning up
68	a real bubbletea program. If you find yourself wanting to mock the
69	broker, you probably want to add a method here, not refactor.
70	
71	### Live broadcasts are "send to everyone, filter on receive"
72	For 推文 (push) updates, `screen_article_view.updatePushInput` calls
73	`broker.SendToAll(senderUID, PushAddedMsg{ArticleID, ...})`. Every
74	connected session receives the message; each article-view model
75	ignores it unless `msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID`. This is simpler than
76	tracking who's viewing what and good enough for our scale. When the
77	filter matches, we **re-fetch from DB** rather than trust the broker
78	payload — that way timestamps and `recommend_score` are always canonical.
79	
80	Water balloons (`broker.Send(toUID, ...)`) target a single user; the
81	multi-session "雙開" case fans out to every live `Session` of that user.
82	
83	### `Store.writeMu` is process-level, not row-level
84	`internal/store/store.go` exposes a single `sync.Mutex` that's grabbed
85	inside every multi-statement repo method (e.g. `PushRepo.Create` runs
86	`INSERT INTO pushes` and `UPDATE articles SET recommend_score` in one
87	transaction under the mutex). This keeps the cached score atomic from
88	the application's perspective and avoids `SQLITE_BUSY` flapping with
89	the pure-Go driver. Don't use `sql.Tx` ad-hoc inside repo methods
90	without also taking `writeMu`. The `pushes` test
91	(`TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`, 50 concurrent inserts) is the
92	canary — if it ever fails under `-race`, the mutex discipline broke.
93	
94	### Migrations live under the store package, not the repo root
95	`internal/store/migrations/*.sql` is embedded with `go:embed
96	migrations/*.sql` from `internal/store/migrate.go`. `go:embed` can't
97	ascend with `..`, so the SQL files live next to the Go code that loads
98	them. Filename format `NNNN_name.sql`; `migrate.apply()` parses the
99	prefix as the version and tracks applied versions in the
100	`schema_migrations` table.
101	
102	### Data deliberately diverges from pttbbs in three places
103	1. **No recno-based article addressing** — `articles.id` (auto-increment)
104	   is opaque and never re-used. Bookmarks stay valid forever.
105	2. **bcrypt cost-12, not `crypt(3)` DES** — `auth.bcryptCost`.
106	3. **No binary shared memory / `userinfo_t` arrays** — presence is a
107	   `sync.RWMutex`-guarded `map[int64][]*Session` in `chat.Broker`.
108	
109	The full mapping (userec_t / boardheader_t / fileheader_t / `.DIR` /
110	推文 / 水球 / userinfo_t) is in `docs/ptt_trace_code/01..06_*.md`.
111	
112	## Testing layers (see `docs/testing.md` for the full doc)
113	
114	- **Layer 1**: plain `go test` for `auth` / `store` / `chat`. Most coverage lives here.
115	- **Layer 2**: direct `tea.Model.Update()` tests in `internal/tui/`. Use
116	  `package tui` (internal), not `tui_test`, to reach unexported screen
117	  constructors. Keybinding-parity tests are table-driven over
118	  `[]string{"esc","backspace","left","h"}` etc. — when adding new keys,
119	  extend the table.
120	- **No `teatest` dependency.** It's experimental (`x/exp`); we test
121	  `Update()` directly with `tea.KeyMsg{...}` synthetic input.
122	- **Always run `-race`.** Several tests (`TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`,
123	  `TestBroker_Concurrency`) exist specifically as race-detector bait.
124	- **Any test needing a DB** uses `storetest.New(t)` which gives a
125	  fresh `t.TempDir()`-backed SQLite. Never share stores across tests.
126	
127	## Conventions worth preserving
128	
129	- **UTF-8 throughout, no Big5.** Use `internal/tui/runewidth.go` helpers
130	  (`PadRight`, `PadLeft`, `Truncate`, `Width`) for any list rendering
131	  that mixes ASCII and CJK — `lipgloss`'s automatic width is unreliable
132	  for double-width glyphs.
133	- **Form screens skip h/l navigation** so the keys remain available for
134	  text editing. Currently: `screen_register`, `screen_post_compose`,
135	  `screen_wb` (compose half).
136	- **NavigateMsg is the only legitimate way to swap screens.** Sub-models
137	  emit it from `Update`; `Root.navigate` is the single switch statement
138	  that constructs the new sub. When you add a new screen, add the case
139	  there and a `Screen*` constant in `messages.go`.
140	- **Window-resize forwarding**: `Root.Update` always forwards
141	  `tea.WindowSizeMsg` to the active sub before its own logic. When
142	  switching to a new sub, `Root.navigate` resends the last known size
143	  so the freshly-mounted screen lays out correctly on first render.
144

150	### Long-term backlog → `TODO.md` + `backlog/`
151	
152	When the user surfaces an idea explicitly **not** being implemented this

157	
158	The bundled `scripts/todo-kanban.sh` validates the format. Run it
159	(`scripts/todo-kanban.sh --validate-only TODO.md`) after editing so syntax
160	drift is caught immediately.
161	
162	#### Three ways to add a TODO entry (preferred order)
163	
164	1. **Structured CLI — `scripts/add-todo.sh`** (default):
165	
166	   ```
167	   scripts/add-todo.sh --priority P3 --effort M \
168	     --title "Title" --description "Description"
169	   ```
170	
171	   Inserts a canonically-formatted line into the right `## P*` lane and
172	   re-runs the validator. Add `--backlog` to also scaffold
173	   `backlog/<slug>.md` from the bundled template.
174	
175	2. **Quick capture — `backlog/inbox.md`** (when priority/effort unclear):
176	
177	   ```
178	   echo "- maybe add docs versioning with mike" >> backlog/inbox.md
179	   ```
180	
181	   When the user asks "sweep the inbox", run
182	   `scripts/sweep-inbox.sh`. It prompts for the missing fields per loose
183	   line and calls `add-todo.sh`. Use `--batch` for non-interactive runs
184	   that only formalize lines with parseable `key=value` pairs.
185	
186	3. **Direct edit of `TODO.md`** — fine if the format is fresh; run
187	   `scripts/todo-kanban.sh --validate-only` afterwards.
188	
189	Add a `backlog/<slug>.md` companion doc when the item meets any of:
190	
191	- carries a `P?` tag (record what was tried so it doesn't need re-investigation)
192	- captures a paused troubleshooting session that you intend to fix later
193	  (preserve the error trace + root cause analysis before context evaporates)
194	- weighs multiple options (record trade-offs, not only the winner)
195	- is `[L]` or `[XL]` (architectural; needs design before code)
196	
197	`[S]` items rarely need a backlog doc — a file path in the `TODO.md` line is
198	usually enough. See [`backlog/README.md`](backlog/README.md) for the full
199	template and "when to add a doc" rules.
200	
201	When implementing a `TODO.md` item, in the same commit:
202	
203	1. Run `scripts/promote-todo.sh --title "<substring>" --summary "<what shipped>"`
204	   to move the entry into `## Done` with the dated syntax and re-validate.
205	2. Mark the corresponding `backlog/<slug>.md` (if any) `Status: shipped`
206	   and keep it as a historical record (don't delete — future-you may
207	   revisit adjacent decisions).
208	
209	`backlog/` is excluded from N/A (no packaging — these files stay in the repo) (see N/A); it
210	is repo metadata for maintainers, not user-facing config to deploy.
211	
212	### Past pitfalls → `pitfalls/`
213	
214	When you spend more than ~15 minutes debugging something that wasn't
215	googleable and the fix is non-obvious, write a `pitfalls/<slug>.md`
216	capturing:
217	
218	1. **Verbatim symptom** — copy-paste error messages exactly, do not
219	   paraphrase (preserves grep-ability for future-you / future agent)
220	2. **Root cause** — why this happens (with source / docs / upstream issue link)
221	3. **Workaround** — copy-pasteable commands or config diff
222	4. **Prevention** — how to avoid stepping on this again
223	
224	Title the doc by the **symptom**, not the root cause (you'll search by what
225	you're seeing, not by what you eventually learned). See
226	[`pitfalls/README.md`](pitfalls/README.md) for the full template and
227	when-to-add rules.
228	
229	**Pitfall vs Hard invariant**: a pitfall *graduates* to a Hard invariant in
230	this file when it (a) recurs across machines/agents/sessions despite being
231	documented, (b) silently corrupts state, or (c) the workaround is non-obvious
232	enough that "remember to do X" isn't safe. When graduating, leave the
233	`pitfalls/<slug>.md` as historical record and link to it from the new
234	invariant.
235	
236	`pitfalls/` is excluded from N/A (no packaging — these files stay in the repo) (see N/A) and
237	**not** auto-redacted; review for secrets before committing.

 `./internal/store/articles.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Article struct {
11		ID              int64
12		BoardID         int64
13		AuthorID        int64
14		AuthorUserID    string
15		Title           string
16		Body            string
17		RecommendScore  int64
18		Filemode        int64
19		CreatedAt       time.Time
20	}
21	
22	type ArticleRepo struct{ s *Store }
23	
24	var (
25		ErrArticleNotFound  = errors.New("article not found")
26		ErrPermissionDenied = errors.New("permission denied")
27	)
28	
29	const articleColumns = `id, board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body,
30		recommend_score, filemode, created_at`
31	
32	func scanArticle(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Article, error) {
33		a := &Article{}
34		err := row.Scan(
35			&a.ID, &a.BoardID, &a.AuthorID, &a.AuthorUserID, &a.Title, &a.Body,
36			&a.RecommendScore, &a.Filemode, &a.CreatedAt,
37		)
38		return a, err
39	}
40	
41	func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoard(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, limit int) ([]*Article, error) {
42		if limit <= 0 {
43			limit = 50
44		}
45		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
46			`SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC LIMIT ?`,
47			boardID, limit,
48		)
49		if err != nil {
50			return nil, err
51		}
52		defer rows.Close()
53		var out []*Article
54		for rows.Next() {
55			a, err := scanArticle(rows)
56			if err != nil {
57				return nil, err
58			}
59			out = append(out, a)
60		}
61		return out, rows.Err()
62	}
63	
64	func (r *ArticleRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Article, error) {
65		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
66		a, err := scanArticle(row)
67		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
68			return nil, ErrArticleNotFound
69		}
70		return a, err
71	}
72	
73	// NeighboursOf returns the article IDs immediately older (prev) and newer
74	// (next) than articleID within the same board, or 0 when there is no
75	// neighbour on that side. "Older" / "newer" are by id (monotonic insert
76	// order); this matches the user-visible "[ / ]" semantics in article view.
77	func (r *ArticleRepo) NeighboursOf(ctx context.Context, boardID, articleID int64) (prev, next int64, err error) {
78		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
79			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id < ? ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1`,
80			boardID, articleID,
81		)
82		if scanErr := row.Scan(&prev); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
83			return 0, 0, scanErr
84		}
85		row = r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
86			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id > ? ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 1`,
87			boardID, articleID,
88		)
89		if scanErr := row.Scan(&next); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
90			return 0, 0, scanErr
91		}
92		return prev, next, nil
93	}
94	
95	func (r *ArticleRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, boardID, authorID int64, authorUserID, title, body string) (*Article, error) {
96		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
97		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
98		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
99			`INSERT INTO articles (board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
100			boardID, authorID, authorUserID, title, body,
101		)
102		if err != nil {
103			return nil, err
104		}
105		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
106		if err != nil {
107			return nil, err
108		}
109		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
110		return scanArticle(row)
111	}
112	
113	// Delete hard-deletes the article. Permission: requester must be the author
114	// OR have a role at-or-above mod. Returns ErrArticleNotFound when the row
115	// is gone, ErrPermissionDenied when the requester is unauthorized.
116	// Cascades to pushes via the FK on pushes.article_id.
117	func (r *ArticleRepo) Delete(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role) error {
118		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
119		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
120		var authorID int64
121		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
122			`SELECT author_id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
123		).Scan(&authorID)
124		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
125			return ErrArticleNotFound
126		}
127		if err != nil {
128			return err
129		}
130		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
131			return ErrPermissionDenied
132		}
133		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID)
134		return err
135	}
136

 `./internal/store/pushes.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// ErrPushNotFound is returned by PushRepo lookups (Delete, future GetByID)
12	// when the row is missing. ErrPermissionDenied is shared with articles.
13	var ErrPushNotFound = errors.New("push not found")
14	
15	type PushKind string
16	
17	const (
18		PushKindPush  PushKind = "push"  // 推 (+1)
19		PushKindBoo   PushKind = "boo"   // 噓 (-1)
20		PushKindArrow PushKind = "arrow" // → (0)
21	)
22	
23	func (k PushKind) Glyph() string {
24		switch k {
25		case PushKindPush:
26			return "推"
27		case PushKindBoo:
28			return "噓"
29		case PushKindArrow:
30			return "→"
31		}
32		return "?"
33	}
34	
35	type Push struct {
36		ID          int64
37		ArticleID   int64
38		UserID      int64
39		UserUserID  string
40		Kind        PushKind
41		Body        string
42		CreatedAt   time.Time
43	}
44	
45	type PushRepo struct{ s *Store }
46	
47	const pushColumns = `id, article_id, user_id, user_userid, kind, body, created_at`
48	
49	func scanPush(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Push, error) {
50		p := &Push{}
51		var kind string
52		if err := row.Scan(&p.ID, &p.ArticleID, &p.UserID, &p.UserUserID, &kind, &p.Body, &p.CreatedAt); err != nil {
53			return nil, err
54		}
55		p.Kind = PushKind(kind)
56		return p, nil
57	}
58	
59	func (r *PushRepo) ListByArticle(ctx context.Context, articleID int64) ([]*Push, error) {
60		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
61			`SELECT `+pushColumns+` FROM pushes WHERE article_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`, articleID,
62		)
63		if err != nil {
64			return nil, err
65		}
66		defer rows.Close()
67		var out []*Push
68		for rows.Next() {
69			p, err := scanPush(rows)
70			if err != nil {
71				return nil, err
72			}
73			out = append(out, p)
74		}
75		return out, rows.Err()
76	}
77	
78	// scoreDelta returns the recommend_score contribution of a push of the
79	// given kind. Positive on add; negate on delete. Centralized so Create
80	// and Delete agree on the algebra.
81	func scoreDelta(kind PushKind) (int, error) {
82		switch kind {
83		case PushKindPush:
84			return 1, nil
85		case PushKindBoo:
86			return -1, nil
87		case PushKindArrow:
88			return 0, nil
89		}
90		return 0, fmt.Errorf("invalid push kind: %q", kind)
91	}
92	
93	// Create inserts a push and updates the cached recommend_score on the
94	// article (push: +1, boo: -1, arrow: 0). Both writes happen under the
95	// store-level writeMu so they're effectively atomic from the app's view;
96	// SQLite's WAL gives us read-consistency for the recompute.
97	func (r *PushRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, articleID, userID int64, userUserID string, kind PushKind, body string) (*Push, error) {
98		delta, err := scoreDelta(kind)
99		if err != nil {
100			return nil, err
101		}
102	
103		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
104		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
105		tx, err := r.s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
106		if err != nil {
107			return nil, err
108		}
109		defer tx.Rollback()
110	
111		res, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
112			`INSERT INTO pushes (article_id, user_id, user_userid, kind, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
113			articleID, userID, userUserID, string(kind), body,
114		)
115		if err != nil {
116			return nil, err
117		}
118		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
119		if err != nil {
120			return nil, err
121		}
122		if delta != 0 {
123			if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
124				`UPDATE articles SET recommend_score = recommend_score + ? WHERE id = ?`,
125				delta, articleID,
126			); err != nil {
127				return nil, err
128			}
129		}
130		if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
131			return nil, err
132		}
133	
134		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+pushColumns+` FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, id)
135		return scanPush(row)
136	}
137	
138	// Delete hard-deletes a push and reverts its score contribution on the
139	// parent article. Permission: requester must be the push author OR have
140	// a role at-or-above mod. Returns ErrPermissionDenied / ErrPushNotFound.
141	// Mirrors Create's writeMu+tx discipline so concurrent deletes can't
142	// double-revert the score.
143	func (r *PushRepo) Delete(ctx context.Context, pushID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role) error {
144		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
145		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
146		tx, err := r.s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
147		if err != nil {
148			return err
149		}
150		defer tx.Rollback()
151	
152		var authorID, articleID int64
153		var kindStr string
154		err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx,
155			`SELECT user_id, article_id, kind FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, pushID,
156		).Scan(&authorID, &articleID, &kindStr)
157		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
158			return ErrPushNotFound
159		}
160		if err != nil {
161			return err
162		}
163		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
164			return ErrPermissionDenied
165		}
166		delta, err := scoreDelta(PushKind(kindStr))
167		if err != nil {
168			return err
169		}
170		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, pushID); err != nil {
171			return err
172		}
173		if delta != 0 {
174			if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
175				`UPDATE articles SET recommend_score = recommend_score - ? WHERE id = ?`,
176				delta, articleID,
177			); err != nil {
178				return err
179			}
180		}
181		return tx.Commit()
182	}
183

68	// SeedDefaults is a belt-and-braces complement to migration 0002. It runs

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/migrate.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"embed"
7		"fmt"
8		"sort"
9		"strconv"
10		"strings"
11	)
12	
13	//go:embed migrations/*.sql
14	var migrationsFS embed.FS
15	
16	// apply runs every migration whose version number isn't already in
17	// schema_migrations. Filenames must be NNNN_name.sql; the leading integer is
18	// the version. Migrations are applied in ascending version order, each in
19	// its own transaction.
20	func apply(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) error {
21		if _, err := db.ExecContext(ctx, `
22			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_migrations (
23				version    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
24				applied_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
25			)
26		`); err != nil {
27			return fmt.Errorf("create schema_migrations: %w", err)
28		}
29	
30		applied, err := loadApplied(ctx, db)
31		if err != nil {
32			return err
33		}
34	
35		entries, err := migrationsFS.ReadDir("migrations")
36		if err != nil {
37			return fmt.Errorf("read embedded migrations: %w", err)
38		}
39		type migration struct {
40			version  int
41			filename string
42		}
43		var pending []migration
44		for _, e := range entries {
45			if e.IsDir() || !strings.HasSuffix(e.Name(), ".sql") {
46				continue
47			}
48			v, err := parseVersion(e.Name())
49			if err != nil {
50				return err
51			}
52			if !applied[v] {
53				pending = append(pending, migration{version: v, filename: e.Name()})
54			}
55		}
56		sort.Slice(pending, func(i, j int) bool { return pending[i].version < pending[j].version })
57	
58		for _, m := range pending {
59			body, err := migrationsFS.ReadFile("migrations/" + m.filename)
60			if err != nil {
61				return fmt.Errorf("read migration %s: %w", m.filename, err)
62			}
63			if err := runOne(ctx, db, m.version, string(body)); err != nil {
64				return fmt.Errorf("apply %s: %w", m.filename, err)
65			}
66		}
67		return nil
68	}
69	
70	func loadApplied(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) (map[int]bool, error) {
71		rows, err := db.QueryContext(ctx, `SELECT version FROM schema_migrations`)
72		if err != nil {
73			return nil, fmt.Errorf("load applied migrations: %w", err)
74		}
75		defer rows.Close()
76		out := map[int]bool{}
77		for rows.Next() {
78			var v int
79			if err := rows.Scan(&v); err != nil {
80				return nil, err
81			}
82			out[v] = true
83		}
84		return out, rows.Err()
85	}
86	
87	func runOne(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB, version int, body string) error {
88		tx, err := db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
89		if err != nil {
90			return err
91		}
92		defer tx.Rollback()
93		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, body); err != nil {
94			return err
95		}
96		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, `INSERT INTO schema_migrations(version) VALUES (?)`, version); err != nil {
97			return err
98		}
99		return tx.Commit()
100	}
101	
102	func parseVersion(filename string) (int, error) {
103		// "0001_init.sql" -> 1
104		idx := strings.IndexByte(filename, '_')
105		if idx <= 0 {
106			return 0, fmt.Errorf("migration filename missing version prefix: %s", filename)
107		}
108		v, err := strconv.Atoi(filename[:idx])
109		if err != nil {
110			return 0, fmt.Errorf("parse version in %s: %w", filename, err)
111		}
112		return v, nil
113	}
114
```

```text
1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"flag"
7		"log"
8		"os"
9		"os/signal"
10		"syscall"
11		"time"
12	
13		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
14		"github.com/charmbracelet/ssh"
15	
16		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/auth"
17		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
18		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/server"
19		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
20		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/version"
21	)
22	
23	func main() {
24		addr := flag.String("addr", ":2222", "SSH listen address")
25		dbPath := flag.String("db", "data/bbs.db", "SQLite database path")
26		hostkey := flag.String("hostkey", ".ssh/host_ed25519", "SSH host key path")
27		adminPassword := flag.String("admin-password", "",
28			"override admin password on first seed; empty uses the baked default 'admin' with must_change_password=1")
29		flag.Parse()
30	
31		st, err := store.Open(*dbPath)
32		if err != nil {
33			log.Fatalf("store.Open: %v", err)
34		}
35		defer st.Close()
36		if err := st.Boards().SeedDefaults(context.Background()); err != nil {
37			log.Fatalf("seed boards: %v", err)
38		}
39		if err := auth.SeedSystemAccounts(context.Background(), st, auth.SeedOpts{
40			AdminPassword: *adminPassword,
41		}); err != nil {
42			log.Fatalf("seed system accounts: %v", err)
43		}
44		log.Printf("storage ready at %s", *dbPath)
45	
46		broker := chat.NewBroker()
47	
48		srv, err := server.New(server.Config{Addr: *addr, HostKey: *hostkey}, st, broker)
49		if err != nil {
50			log.Fatalf("server.New: %v", err)
51		}
52	
53		ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
54		defer stop()
55	
56		errCh := make(chan error, 1)
57		go func() {
58			log.Printf("sshbbs %s listening on %s", version.Version, *addr)
59			errCh <- srv.ListenAndServe()
60		}()
61	
62		select {
63		case err := <-errCh:
64			if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, ssh.ErrServerClosed) {
65				log.Fatalf("listen: %v", err)
66			}
67		case <-ctx.Done():
68			log.Println("shutdown signal received")
69			shutdown(srv, broker)
70		}
71	}
72	
73	// shutdown closes the listener (refuses new connections), then asks every
74	// live bubbletea program to quit, then closes the SSH server hard. The DB
75	// closes on the deferred st.Close() in main.
76	func shutdown(srv *ssh.Server, broker *chat.Broker) {
77		const drainTimeout = 3 * time.Second
78	
79		// 1. Stop accepting new connections.
80		listenerCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), drainTimeout)
81		defer cancel()
82		if err := srv.Shutdown(listenerCtx); err != nil {
83			log.Printf("server.Shutdown: %v", err)
84		}
85	
86		// 2. Tell every active session to quit.
87		sessions := broker.SessionsSnapshot()
88		if len(sessions) == 0 {
89			return
90		}
91		log.Printf("draining %d active session(s)", len(sessions))
92		for _, s := range sessions {
93			s.Program.Send(tea.Quit())
94		}
95	
96		// 3. Give them a moment to flush their final view, then force-close.
97		timer := time.NewTimer(drainTimeout)
98		defer timer.Stop()
99		for {
100			if len(broker.SessionsSnapshot()) == 0 {
101				log.Println("all sessions drained")
102				return
103			}
104			select {
105			case <-timer.C:
106				log.Printf("drain timeout; %d session(s) still attached", len(broker.SessionsSnapshot()))
107				return
108			case <-time.After(100 * time.Millisecond):
109			}
110		}
111	}
112
```

```text
1	-- Initial schema for the SSH BBS.
2	-- All tables use auto-increment INTEGER PRIMARY KEY plus a unique business
3	-- key where appropriate. We deliberately do NOT mimic pttbbs's recno-based
4	-- addressing (a holdover from mmap'd binary .DIR files) — a relational DB
5	-- removes the recno-tombstone fragility documented in pttbbs.
6	
7	PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
8	
9	CREATE TABLE users (
10	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
11	    user_id         TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- userec_t.userid
12	    password_hash   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- bcrypt; replaces userec_t.passwd (DES)
13	    nickname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.nickname
14	    realname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.realname
15	    email           TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.email
16	    num_logins      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numlogindays (loose)
17	    num_posts       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numposts
18	    last_login_at   DATETIME,                            -- userec_t.lastlogin
19	    last_host       TEXT,                                -- userec_t.lasthost
20	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
21	);
22	
23	CREATE TABLE boards (
24	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
25	    name            TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- boardheader_t.brdname
26	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- boardheader_t.title
27	    description     TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
28	    bm              TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- boardheader_t.BM (comma-sep)
29	    attr            INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- boardheader_t.brdattr (P1+)
30	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
31	);
32	
33	CREATE TABLE articles (
34	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
35	    board_id        INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES boards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
36	    author_id       INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
37	    author_userid   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized (fileheader_t.owner)
38	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- fileheader_t.title
39	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
40	    recommend_score INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- cached sum of pushes (+1) - boos (-1)
41	    filemode        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- fileheader_t.filemode (P1+)
42	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
43	);
44	CREATE INDEX idx_articles_board_created ON articles(board_id, created_at DESC);
45	
46	CREATE TABLE pushes (
47	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
48	    article_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES articles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
49	    user_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
50	    user_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
51	    kind            TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (kind IN ('push','boo','arrow')), -- 推/噓/→
52	    body            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
53	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
54	);
55	CREATE INDEX idx_pushes_article ON pushes(article_id, id);
56	
57	CREATE TABLE water_balloons (
58	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
59	    from_user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
60	    from_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
61	    to_user_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
62	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
63	    delivered_live  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
64	    read_at         DATETIME,
65	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
66	);
67	CREATE INDEX idx_wb_to_unread ON water_balloons(to_user_id, read_at);
68

1	package tui
2	
3	// Screen identifies which sub-model the Root should be showing.
4	type Screen int
5	
6	const (
7		ScreenMainMenu Screen = iota
8		ScreenBoardList
9		ScreenBoardView
10		ScreenArticleView
11		ScreenPostCompose
12		ScreenWBInbox
13		ScreenWBCompose
14		ScreenOnline
15		ScreenMailInbox
16		ScreenMailThread
17		ScreenMailCompose
18		ScreenPasswordChange
19		ScreenAdminUsers
20	)
21	
22	// NavigateMsg is emitted by sub-screens to ask the Root to swap screens.
23	// Optional fields carry context the destination screen needs.
24	type NavigateMsg struct {
25		To           Screen
26		BoardID      int64
27		ArticleID    int64
28		Recipient    string // for ScreenWBCompose / ScreenMailCompose: prefilled recipient userid
29		MailID       int64  // for ScreenMailCompose: parent mail id when replying
30		MailThreadID int64  // for ScreenMailThread: which thread to open
31	}
32	
33	// ErrorMsg surfaces an error as a transient toast.
34	type ErrorMsg struct{ Err error }
35	
36	// WBIncomingMsg is delivered by the chat broker (or replayed from DB on Init)
37	// to notify the recipient that a new water balloon arrived.
38	type WBIncomingMsg struct {
39		ID         int64
40		FromUserID string
41		Body       string
42	}
43	
44	// PushAddedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when a push is added to an
45	// article. The article-view model checks ArticleID to decide whether to
46	// re-render.
47	type PushAddedMsg struct {
48		ArticleID  int64
49		UserUserID string
50		Kind       string
51		Body       string
52	}
53	
54	// MailIncomingMsg notifies the recipient that a new mail has arrived.
55	// Unlike WBIncomingMsg it does NOT auto-toast — mail is asynchronous; the
56	// open mail inbox can choose to refresh, and the main menu reads the unread
57	// counter from DB.
58	type MailIncomingMsg struct {
59		ID         int64
60		ThreadID   int64
61		FromUserID string
62		Subject    string
63	}
64	
65	// ArticleAddedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when a new article is
66	// posted. Board-view models filter by BoardID and re-fetch the article
67	// list from DB so timestamps and ordering reflect canonical state.
68	type ArticleAddedMsg struct {
69		BoardID      int64
70		ArticleID    int64
71		AuthorUserID string
72		Title        string
73	}
74
```

 `./internal/tui/root.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"time"
7	
8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
12	)
13	
14	// Deps bundles everything every screen may need. Pass-by-value into
15	// constructors keeps screens self-contained.
16	type Deps struct {
17		Store              *store.Store
18		Broker             *chat.Broker // nil during register
19		User               *store.User  // nil iff IsRegister
20		IsRegister         bool
21		MustChangePassword bool // true iff user must rotate password before reaching the main menu
22	}
23	
24	// Root is the top-level tea.Model. It owns the active sub-model and
25	// handles cross-cutting concerns: window size, global hotkeys, navigation,
26	// and the toast footer.
27	type Root struct {
28		deps   Deps
29		sub    tea.Model
30		width  int
31		height int
32	
33		toast      string
34		toastUntil time.Time
35	}
36	
37	const toastDuration = 3 * time.Second
38	
39	// NewRoot builds the Root with the right initial sub-screen.
40	//
41	// Priority: IsRegister wins over MustChangePassword wins over the regular
42	// authenticated main menu — these flags are mutually exclusive in practice
43	// (set by the SSH password callback / makeProgramHandler).
44	func NewRoot(deps Deps) Root {
45		r := Root{deps: deps}
46		switch {
47		case deps.IsRegister:
48			r.sub = newRegisterModel(deps)
49		case deps.MustChangePassword:
50			r.sub = newPasswordChangeModel(deps)
51		default:
52			r.sub = newMainMenuModel(deps)
53		}
54		return r
55	}
56	
57	func (m Root) Init() tea.Cmd {
58		return m.sub.Init()
59	}
60	
61	func (m Root) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
62		switch msg := msg.(type) {
63		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
64			m.width = msg.Width
65			m.height = msg.Height
66			var cmd tea.Cmd
67			m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
68			return m, cmd
69	
70		case tea.KeyMsg:
71			// Global ctrl+c always quits.
72			if msg.String() == "ctrl+c" {
73				return m, tea.Quit
74			}
75			// Global Ctrl+U opens water balloon inbox (logged-in only).
76			if msg.String() == "ctrl+u" && !m.deps.IsRegister {
77				return m.navigate(NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox})
78			}
79	
80		case NavigateMsg:
81			return m.navigate(msg)
82	
83		case ErrorMsg:
84			if msg.Err != nil {
85				m.setToast("⚠ " + msg.Err.Error())
86			}
87			return m, nil
88	
89		case WBIncomingMsg:
90			m.setToast("💧 " + msg.FromUserID + ": " + msg.Body)
91			// Mark this WB as read so it doesn't replay on reconnect.
92			if m.deps.Store != nil && msg.ID != 0 {
93				go func(id int64) {
94					_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), id)
95				}(msg.ID)
96			}
97			// fall through so the active screen can also react
98		}
99	
100		var cmd tea.Cmd
101		m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
102		return m, cmd
103	}
104	
105	func (m Root) View() string {
106		v := m.sub.View()
107		if m.toast != "" && time.Now().Before(m.toastUntil) {
108			v += "\n" + StyleToast.Render(m.toast)
109		}
110		return v
111	}
112	
113	func (m *Root) setToast(s string) {
114		m.toast = s
115		m.toastUntil = time.Now().Add(toastDuration)
116	}
117	
118	// guestWriteBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a guest is trying to enter
119	// a write-capable screen, otherwise nil. Layer 1 of the read-only path.
120	func (m Root) guestWriteBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
121		if m.deps.User == nil || m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest {
122			return nil
123		}
124		switch to {
125		case ScreenPostCompose, ScreenWBCompose, ScreenMailCompose, ScreenAdminUsers:
126			return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("guest 為唯讀帳號 (read-only)")} }
127		}
128		return nil
129	}
130	
131	// navigateNonAdminBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a non-admin tries
132	// to reach the admin user-management screen.
133	func (m Root) navigateNonAdminBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
134		if to != ScreenAdminUsers {
135			return nil
136		}
137		if m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role == store.RoleAdmin {
138			return nil
139		}
140		return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("管理員專用 (admin only)")} }
141	}
142	
143	func (m Root) navigate(n NavigateMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
144		if cmd := m.guestWriteBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
145			return m, cmd
146		}
147		if cmd := m.navigateNonAdminBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
148			return m, cmd
149		}
150		var sub tea.Model
151		switch n.To {
152		case ScreenMainMenu:
153			sub = newMainMenuModel(m.deps)
154		case ScreenBoardList:
155			sub = newBoardListModel(m.deps)
156		case ScreenBoardView:
157			sub = newBoardViewModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
158		case ScreenArticleView:
159			sub = newArticleViewModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
160		case ScreenPostCompose:
161			sub = newPostComposeModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
162		case ScreenWBInbox:
163			sub = newWBInboxModel(m.deps)
164		case ScreenWBCompose:
165			sub = newWBComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient)
166		case ScreenOnline:
167			sub = newOnlineModel(m.deps)
168		case ScreenMailInbox:
169			sub = newMailInboxModel(m.deps)
170		case ScreenMailThread:
171			sub = newMailThreadModel(m.deps, n.MailThreadID)
172		case ScreenMailCompose:
173			sub = newMailComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient, n.MailID)
174		case ScreenPasswordChange:
175			sub = newPasswordChangeModel(m.deps)
176		case ScreenAdminUsers:
177			sub = newAdminUsersModel(m.deps)
178		default:
179			// Unknown screen — leave sub untouched. Later steps add cases.
180			return m, nil
181		}
182		m.sub = sub
183		cmd := sub.Init()
184		if m.width > 0 {
185			// Forward current size to the new sub so it can lay out immediately.
186			var cmd2 tea.Cmd
187			m.sub, cmd2 = m.sub.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: m.width, Height: m.height})
188			if cmd2 != nil {
189				cmd = tea.Batch(cmd, cmd2)
190			}
191		}
192		return m, cmd
193	}
194
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./internal/tui/screen_article_view.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
9		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
12	)
13	
14	type articleViewModel struct {
15		deps    Deps
16		article *store.Article
17		pushes  []*store.Push
18		width   int
19		height  int
20		scroll  int
21		loadErr error
22	
23		// push input state
24		pushing  bool
25		pushKind store.PushKind
26		pushIn   textinput.Model
27		err      string
28	
29		// delete state. pendingDelete: showing the y/N confirm overlay.
30		// pushCursor: -1 = no push selected (D targets article); 0..len-1 = D targets that push.
31		pendingDelete bool
32		pushCursor    int
33	}
34	
35	func newArticleViewModel(deps Deps, articleID int64) articleViewModel {
36		ctx := context.Background()
37		a, err := deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, articleID)
38		if err != nil {
39			return articleViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
40		}
41		pushes, err := deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, articleID)
42	
43		ti := textinput.New()
44		ti.Placeholder = "comment…"
45		ti.CharLimit = 80
46		ti.Width = 60
47	
48		return articleViewModel{deps: deps, article: a, pushes: pushes, loadErr: err, pushIn: ti, pushCursor: -1}
49	}
50	
51	// canDeleteArticle returns true if the current user may delete the loaded
52	// article (author, or mod-or-above). Used both as a permission gate at
53	// the key handler and to decide whether to render the help-line hint.
54	func (m articleViewModel) canDeleteArticle() bool {
55		if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
56			return false
57		}
58		if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
59			return true
60		}
61		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
62	}
63	
64	// canDeletePush mirrors canDeleteArticle for the push at index idx.
65	func (m articleViewModel) canDeletePush(idx int) bool {
66		if m.deps.User == nil || idx < 0 || idx >= len(m.pushes) {
67			return false
68		}
69		if m.deps.User.ID == m.pushes[idx].UserID {
70			return true
71		}
72		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
73	}
74	
75	// canPush returns true if the current user is allowed to add new pushes.
76	// Guests can read but not write.
77	func (m articleViewModel) canPush() bool {
78		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest
79	}
80	
81	func (m articleViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
82	
83	func (m articleViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
84		switch msg := msg.(type) {
85		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
86			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
87			return m, nil
88	
89		case PushAddedMsg:
90			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
91				ctx := context.Background()
92				// Re-fetch from DB so timestamps and recommend_score reflect
93				// the canonical state, not the just-synthesized broker payload.
94				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
95					m.pushes = pushes
96				}
97				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
98					m.article = a
99				}
100			}
101			return m, nil
102	
103		case tea.KeyMsg:
104			if m.pushing {
105				return m.updatePushInput(msg)
106			}
107			if m.pendingDelete {
108				return m.updateDeleteConfirm(msg)
109			}
110			switch msg.String() {
111			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
112				boardID := int64(0)
113				if m.article != nil {
114					boardID = m.article.BoardID
115				}
116				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: boardID} }
117			case "Q":
118				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
119			case "up", "k":
120				if m.scroll > 0 {
121					m.scroll--
122				}
123			case "down", "j":
124				m.scroll++
125			case "pgup", "b":
126				m.scroll = max(0, m.scroll-10)
127			case "pgdown", " ":
128				m.scroll += 10
129			case "g":
130				m.scroll = 0
131			case "G":
132				m.scroll = max(0, m.bodyLineCount()-m.viewportLines())
133			case "[":
134				if m.article == nil {
135					return m, nil
136				}
137				prev, _, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().NeighboursOf(context.Background(), m.article.BoardID, m.article.ID)
138				if err != nil || prev == 0 {
139					return m, nil
140				}
141				return m, func() tea.Msg {
142					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: prev, BoardID: m.article.BoardID}
143				}
144			case "]":
145				if m.article == nil {
146					return m, nil
147				}
148				_, next, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().NeighboursOf(context.Background(), m.article.BoardID, m.article.ID)
149				if err != nil || next == 0 {
150					return m, nil
151				}
152				return m, func() tea.Msg {
153					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: next, BoardID: m.article.BoardID}
154				}
155			case "+":
156				if !m.canPush() {
157					return m, nil
158				}
159				return m.openPush(store.PushKindPush), nil
160			case "-":
161				if !m.canPush() {
162					return m, nil
163				}
164				return m.openPush(store.PushKindBoo), nil
165			case "=":
166				if !m.canPush() {
167					return m, nil
168				}
169				return m.openPush(store.PushKindArrow), nil
170			case "p":
171				// Advance the push cursor. -1 → 0 → ... → len-1 → -1 (cycles
172				// back to "no selection, D targets the article").
173				if len(m.pushes) == 0 {
174					return m, nil
175				}
176				m.pushCursor++
177				if m.pushCursor >= len(m.pushes) {
178					m.pushCursor = -1
179				}
180				m.err = ""
181				return m, nil
182			case "P":
183				if len(m.pushes) == 0 {
184					return m, nil
185				}
186				if m.pushCursor < 0 {
187					m.pushCursor = len(m.pushes) - 1
188				} else {
189					m.pushCursor--
190				}
191				m.err = ""
192				return m, nil
193			case "D":
194				if m.pushCursor >= 0 {
195					if !m.canDeletePush(m.pushCursor) {
196						m.err = "權限不足 (only the push author or a mod can delete it)"
197						return m, nil
198					}
199				} else {
200					if !m.canDeleteArticle() {
201						m.err = "權限不足 (only the author or a mod can delete)"
202						return m, nil
203					}
204				}
205				m.pendingDelete = true
206				m.err = ""
207				return m, nil
208			}
209		}
210		return m, nil
211	}
212	
213	// updateDeleteConfirm handles y/n while the confirm overlay is up.
214	// y: dispatch the delete; if targeting a push, refresh the push list
215	// and the article (for the score). If targeting the article, navigate
216	// back to the board view.
217	// n / esc: cancel.
218	func (m articleViewModel) updateDeleteConfirm(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
219		switch msg.String() {
220		case "y", "Y":
221			if m.article == nil || m.deps.User == nil {
222				m.pendingDelete = false
223				return m, nil
224			}
225			ctx := context.Background()
226			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.pushCursor < len(m.pushes) {
227				pushID := m.pushes[m.pushCursor].ID
228				if err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().Delete(ctx, pushID, m.deps.User.ID, m.deps.User.Role); err != nil {
229					m.err = err.Error()
230					m.pendingDelete = false
231					return m, nil
232				}
233				// Refresh the push list and re-fetch the article so its
234				// recommend_score reflects the reverted delta.
235				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
236					m.pushes = pushes
237				}
238				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
239					m.article = a
240				}
241				// Clamp the cursor so the next D / p continues from a valid index.
242				if m.pushCursor >= len(m.pushes) {
243					m.pushCursor = -1
244				}
245				m.pendingDelete = false
246				return m, nil
247			}
248			if err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Delete(ctx, m.article.ID, m.deps.User.ID, m.deps.User.Role); err != nil {
249				m.err = err.Error()
250				m.pendingDelete = false
251				return m, nil
252			}
253			boardID := m.article.BoardID
254			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: boardID} }
255		case "n", "N", "esc":
256			m.pendingDelete = false
257			return m, nil
258		}
259		return m, nil
260	}
261	
262	// bodyLineCount returns the number of lines View will render for the
263	// loaded article (matches strings.Split(body, "\n") used in View).
264	func (m articleViewModel) bodyLineCount() int {
265		if m.article == nil {
266			return 0
267		}
268		return len(strings.Split(m.article.Body, "\n"))
269	}
270	
271	// viewportLines mirrors the maxLines computation in View so g/G/PgDn
272	// agree on how many lines fit on screen.
273	func (m articleViewModel) viewportLines() int {
274		return max(m.height-16, 5)
275	}
276	
277	func (m articleViewModel) openPush(k store.PushKind) tea.Model {
278		m.pushing = true
279		m.pushKind = k
280		m.pushIn.SetValue("")
281		m.pushIn.Focus()
282		m.err = ""
283		return m
284	}
285	
286	func (m articleViewModel) updatePushInput(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
287		switch msg.String() {
288		case "esc":
289			m.pushing = false
290			m.pushIn.Blur()
291			return m, nil
292		case "enter":
293			body := strings.TrimSpace(m.pushIn.Value())
294			if body == "" && m.pushKind != store.PushKindArrow {
295				m.err = "comment required for 推/噓"
296				return m, nil
297			}
298			ctx := context.Background()
299			u := m.deps.User
300			p, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().Create(ctx, m.article.ID, u.ID, u.UserID, m.pushKind, body)
301			if err != nil {
302				m.err = err.Error()
303				return m, nil
304			}
305			// Append locally so the originator sees it immediately.
306			m.pushes = append(m.pushes, p)
307			if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
308				m.article = a
309			}
310			m.pushing = false
311			m.pushIn.Blur()
312			// Broadcast to other live sessions; they filter by ArticleID.
313			if m.deps.Broker != nil {
314				m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, PushAddedMsg{
315					ArticleID:  m.article.ID,
316					UserUserID: u.UserID,
317					Kind:       string(m.pushKind),
318					Body:       body,
319				})
320			}
321			return m, nil
322		}
323		var cmd tea.Cmd
324		m.pushIn, cmd = m.pushIn.Update(msg)
325		return m, cmd
326	}
327	
328	func (m articleViewModel) View() string {
329		if m.loadErr != nil {
330			return "\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n"
331		}
332		if m.article == nil {
333			return "\n  " + StyleDim.Render("(no article)") + "\n"
334		}
335		a := m.article
336	
337		var b strings.Builder
338		b.WriteString("\n")
339		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 文章 #%d · %s ", a.ID, a.AuthorUserID)))
340		b.WriteString("\n\n")
341	
342		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title: ") + a.Title + "\n")
343		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Date:  ") + a.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05") + "\n")
344		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Score: ") + fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore) + "\n")
345		b.WriteString("\n")
346	
347		bodyLines := strings.Split(a.Body, "\n")
348		start := min(m.scroll, len(bodyLines))
349		maxLines := max(m.height-16, 5)
350		end := min(start+maxLines, len(bodyLines))
351		for _, line := range bodyLines[start:end] {
352			b.WriteString("  " + line + "\n")
353		}
354		if start > 0 || end < len(bodyLines) {
355			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("[lines %d-%d / %d]", start+1, end, len(bodyLines))))
356			b.WriteString("\n")
357		}
358	
359		if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
360			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("── 推文 (%d) ──", len(m.pushes))) + "\n")
361			for i, p := range m.pushes {
362				ts := p.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04")
363				gutter := "  "
364				if i == m.pushCursor {
365					gutter = "▸ "
366				}
367				line := fmt.Sprintf("%s%s %s %s  %s",
368					gutter,
369					renderPushKind(p.Kind),
370					PadRight(p.UserUserID, 14),
371					p.Body,
372					StyleDim.Render(ts),
373				)
374				if i == m.pushCursor {
375					line = StyleHighlight.Render(line)
376				}
377				b.WriteString(line + "\n")
378			}
379		}
380	
381		if m.pushing {
382			b.WriteString("\n  ")
383			b.WriteString(renderPushKind(m.pushKind) + " ")
384			b.WriteString(m.pushIn.View())
385			b.WriteString("\n")
386			if m.err != "" {
387				b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
388			}
389			b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("Enter send · Esc cancel"))
390		} else if m.pendingDelete {
391			prompt := "確定刪除這篇文章? (y/N)"
392			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.pushCursor < len(m.pushes) {
393				prompt = fmt.Sprintf("確定刪除推文 #%d? (y/N)", m.pushCursor+1)
394			}
395			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+prompt))
396		} else {
397			help := "j/k scroll"
398			if m.canPush() {
399				help += " · + 推 · - 噓 · = →"
400			}
401			if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
402				help += " · p/P 選推文"
403			}
404			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.canDeletePush(m.pushCursor) {
405				help += " · D 刪除推文"
406			} else if m.canDeleteArticle() {
407				help += " · D 刪除文章"
408			}
409			help += " · Esc/← back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
410			if m.err != "" {
411				b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err))
412			}
413			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
414		}
415		b.WriteString("\n")
416		return b.String()
417	}
418	
419	func renderPushKind(k store.PushKind) string {
420		switch k {
421		case store.PushKindPush:
422			return StylePushKind.Render("推")
423		case store.PushKindBoo:
424			return StyleBooKind.Render("噓")
425		case store.PushKindArrow:
426			return StyleArrowKind.Render("→")
427		}
428		return "?"
429	}
430

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textarea"
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
9		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
10	)
11	
12	const (
13		composeFocusTitle = iota
14		composeFocusBody
15	)
16	
17	type postComposeModel struct {
18		deps    Deps
19		boardID int64
20		title   textinput.Model
21		body    textarea.Model
22		focus   int
23		width   int
24		height  int
25		err     string
26	}
27	
28	func newPostComposeModel(deps Deps, boardID int64) postComposeModel {
29		ti := textinput.New()
30		ti.Placeholder = "標題 title"
31		ti.CharLimit = 64
32		ti.Width = 60
33		ti.Focus()
34	
35		ta := textarea.New()
36		ta.Placeholder = "內容 body…"
37		ta.CharLimit = 8000
38		ta.SetWidth(72)
39		ta.SetHeight(12)
40	
41		return postComposeModel{
42			deps:    deps,
43			boardID: boardID,
44			title:   ti,
45			body:    ta,
46			focus:   composeFocusTitle,
47		}
48	}
49	
50	func (m postComposeModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
51	
52	func (m postComposeModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
53		switch msg := msg.(type) {
54		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
55			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
56			bodyW := max(40, msg.Width-8)
57			bodyH := max(8, msg.Height-12)
58			m.body.SetWidth(bodyW)
59			m.body.SetHeight(bodyH)
60			m.title.Width = max(20, msg.Width-12)
61			return m, nil
62	
63		case tea.KeyMsg:
64			switch msg.String() {
65			case "esc":
66				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: m.boardID} }
67			case "tab":
68				m.toggleFocus()
69				return m, nil
70			case "ctrl+s":
71				return m.submit()
72			}
73		}
74	
75		var cmd tea.Cmd
76		if m.focus == composeFocusTitle {
77			// In title field, Enter advances to body.
78			if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok && k.String() == "enter" {
79				m.toggleFocus()
80				return m, nil
81			}
82			m.title, cmd = m.title.Update(msg)
83		} else {
84			m.body, cmd = m.body.Update(msg)
85		}
86		return m, cmd
87	}
88	
89	func (m *postComposeModel) toggleFocus() {
90		if m.focus == composeFocusTitle {
91			m.focus = composeFocusBody
92			m.title.Blur()
93			m.body.Focus()
94		} else {
95			m.focus = composeFocusTitle
96			m.body.Blur()
97			m.title.Focus()
98		}
99	}
100	
101	func (m postComposeModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
102		title := strings.TrimSpace(m.title.Value())
103		body := m.body.Value()
104		if title == "" {
105			m.err = "title is required"
106			return m, nil
107		}
108		if strings.TrimSpace(body) == "" {
109			m.err = "body is required"
110			return m, nil
111		}
112		ctx := context.Background()
113		u := m.deps.User
114		art, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Create(ctx, m.boardID, u.ID, u.UserID, title, body)
115		if err != nil {
116			m.err = err.Error()
117			return m, nil
118		}
119		if err := m.deps.Store.Users().IncrementPosts(ctx, u.ID); err != nil {
120			m.err = err.Error()
121			return m, nil
122		}
123		// Broadcast to other live sessions; recipients filter by BoardID and
124		// re-fetch the canonical list (mirrors the PushAddedMsg pattern).
125		if m.deps.Broker != nil {
126			m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, ArticleAddedMsg{
127				BoardID:      m.boardID,
128				ArticleID:    art.ID,
129				AuthorUserID: u.UserID,
130				Title:        art.Title,
131			})
132		}
133		return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: m.boardID} }
134	}
135	
136	func (m postComposeModel) View() string {
137		var b strings.Builder
138		b.WriteString("\n")
139		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 發表新文章 New Post "))
140		b.WriteString("\n\n")
141	
142		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title:") + "\n")
143		b.WriteString("  " + m.title.View() + "\n\n")
144	
145		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Body:") + "\n")
146		b.WriteString("  " + indent(m.body.View(), "  ") + "\n")
147	
148		if m.err != "" {
149			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
150		}
151		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab switch field · Enter (in title) → body · Ctrl+S send · Esc cancel"))
152		b.WriteString("\n")
153		return b.String()
154	}
155	
156	func indent(s, prefix string) string {
157		lines := strings.Split(s, "\n")
158		for i, ln := range lines {
159			if i == 0 {
160				continue
161			}
162			lines[i] = prefix + ln
163		}
164		return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
165	}

```text
1	-- Add role + must_change_password to users.
2	-- role is a four-tier enum (guest < user < mod < admin); existing rows
3	-- pick up DEFAULT 'user' which preserves prior behavior.
4	-- must_change_password is set to 1 when seeding the bootstrapped admin
5	-- account on first run; the password-change screen clears it on success.
6	
7	ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'user'
8	    CHECK (role IN ('guest','user','mod','admin'));
9	ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN must_change_password INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0;
10	CREATE INDEX idx_users_role ON users(role);
11

1	package auth
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"fmt"
7		"regexp"
8		"strings"
9	
10		"golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
13	)
14	
15	// Reserved SSH usernames. `new` triggers the in-TUI register flow;
16	// `guest` short-circuits to a read-only spectator account; `admin` is
17	// the bootstrapped administrator and may not be re-registered.
18	const (
19		ReservedUsernameNew   = "new"
20		ReservedUsernameGuest = "guest"
21		ReservedUsernameAdmin = "admin"
22	)
23	
24	// IsReservedUsername reports whether s matches one of the three reserved
25	// SSH usernames (case-insensitive). Used by Register to refuse the names
26	// and by the SSH password callback to dispatch to the right flow.
27	func IsReservedUsername(s string) bool {
28		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
29		return strings.EqualFold(s, ReservedUsernameNew) ||
30			strings.EqualFold(s, ReservedUsernameGuest) ||
31			strings.EqualFold(s, ReservedUsernameAdmin)
32	}
33	
34	const (
35		bcryptCost     = 12
36		minPasswordLen = 6
37		maxPasswordLen = 128
38		maxNickname    = 32
39		maxEmail       = 128
40	)
41	
42	var (
43		ErrUserExists       = errors.New("user already exists")
44		ErrInvalidUserID    = errors.New("user id must be 3-12 chars, start with a letter, and use only letters/digits/underscore")
45		ErrInvalidPassword  = fmt.Errorf("password must be %d-%d characters", minPasswordLen, maxPasswordLen)
46		ErrNicknameTooLong  = fmt.Errorf("nickname must be %d characters or fewer", maxNickname)
47		ErrEmailTooLong     = fmt.Errorf("email must be %d characters or fewer", maxEmail)
48		ErrReservedUsername = fmt.Errorf("user id is reserved (%q / %q / %q)",
49			ReservedUsernameNew, ReservedUsernameGuest, ReservedUsernameAdmin)
50		ErrBadCredentials   = errors.New("invalid user id or password")
51	)
52	
53	var userIDRe = regexp.MustCompile(`^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_]{2,11}$`)
54	
55	// ValidatePassword enforces the length rules. Used by Register and the
56	// password-change screen so the rules don't drift between call sites.
57	func ValidatePassword(password string) error {
58		if l := len(password); l < minPasswordLen || l > maxPasswordLen {
59			return ErrInvalidPassword
60		}
61		return nil
62	}
63	
64	// HashPassword returns a bcrypt hash at the package's standard cost.
65	// Callers outside auth (TUI, seed) use this so bcryptCost stays in one place.
66	func HashPassword(password string) (string, error) {
67		hash, err := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(password), bcryptCost)
68		if err != nil {
69			return "", fmt.Errorf("hash password: %w", err)
70		}
71		return string(hash), nil
72	}
73	
74	// VerifyPasswordHash returns nil when the hash matches the password.
75	// Callers outside auth use this instead of importing bcrypt directly.
76	func VerifyPasswordHash(hash, password string) error {
77		return bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte(hash), []byte(password))
78	}
79	
80	// Register validates inputs, hashes the password with bcrypt, and inserts a
81	// new user. Returns ErrUserExists on UNIQUE conflict.
82	func Register(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, userID, password, nickname, email string) (*store.User, error) {
83		userID = strings.TrimSpace(userID)
84		nickname = strings.TrimSpace(nickname)
85		email = strings.TrimSpace(email)
86	
87		if !userIDRe.MatchString(userID) {
88			return nil, ErrInvalidUserID
89		}
90		if IsReservedUsername(userID) {
91			return nil, ErrReservedUsername
92		}
93		if err := ValidatePassword(password); err != nil {
94			return nil, err
95		}
96		if len([]rune(nickname)) > maxNickname {
97			return nil, ErrNicknameTooLong
98		}
99		if len(email) > maxEmail {
100			return nil, ErrEmailTooLong
101		}
102	
103		hash, err := HashPassword(password)
104		if err != nil {
105			return nil, err
106		}
107	
108		u, err := st.Users().Create(ctx, userID, hash, nickname, email)
109		if err != nil {
110			// modernc.org/sqlite returns a wrapped UNIQUE error; check the text.
111			if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "UNIQUE") {
112				return nil, ErrUserExists
113			}
114			return nil, err
115		}
116		return u, nil
117	}
118	
119	// VerifyLogin returns the user record if the password matches, ErrBadCredentials otherwise.
120	// On success, NoteLogin is called to update last_login_at / last_host / num_logins.
121	func VerifyLogin(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, userID, password, host string) (*store.User, error) {
122		u, err := st.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, strings.TrimSpace(userID))
123		if err != nil {
124			if errors.Is(err, store.ErrUserNotFound) {
125				// Run a dummy bcrypt check anyway to even out timing.
126				_ = bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte("$2a$12$invalidinvalidinvalidinvalidinvalidinvalidinvalidinvali"), []byte(password))
127				return nil, ErrBadCredentials
128			}
129			return nil, err
130		}
131		if err := bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte(u.PasswordHash), []byte(password)); err != nil {
132			return nil, ErrBadCredentials
133		}
134		// Skip NoteLogin for guest — the row is shared across every spectator
135		// session, so num_logins / last_login_at are meaningless and the write
136		// would just create contention. Defensive: the SSH password callback
137		// short-circuits guest before calling VerifyLogin in normal flow.
138		if u.Role == store.RoleGuest {
139			return u, nil
140		}
141		if err := st.Users().NoteLogin(ctx, u.ID, host); err != nil {
142			return nil, fmt.Errorf("note login: %w", err)
143		}
144		// Re-fetch so callers see the updated last_login_at / num_logins.
145		return st.Users().GetByID(ctx, u.ID)
146	}
147
```
</details></tool-use>
