 `./internal/store/pushes.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// ErrPushNotFound is returned by PushRepo lookups (Delete, future GetByID)
12	// when the row is missing. ErrPermissionDenied is shared with articles.
13	var ErrPushNotFound = errors.New("push not found")
14	
15	type PushKind string
16	
17	const (
18		PushKindPush  PushKind = "push"  // 推 (+1)
19		PushKindBoo   PushKind = "boo"   // 噓 (-1)
20		PushKindArrow PushKind = "arrow" // → (0)
21	)
22	
23	func (k PushKind) Glyph() string {
24		switch k {
25		case PushKindPush:
26			return "推"
27		case PushKindBoo:
28			return "噓"
29		case PushKindArrow:
30			return "→"
31		}
32		return "?"
33	}
34	
35	type Push struct {
36		ID          int64
37		ArticleID   int64
38		UserID      int64
39		UserUserID  string
40		Kind        PushKind
41		Body        string
42		CreatedAt   time.Time
43	}
44	
45	type PushRepo struct{ s *Store }
46	
47	const pushColumns = `id, article_id, user_id, user_userid, kind, body, created_at`
48	
49	func scanPush(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Push, error) {
50		p := &Push{}
51		var kind string
52		if err := row.Scan(&p.ID, &p.ArticleID, &p.UserID, &p.UserUserID, &kind, &p.Body, &p.CreatedAt); err != nil {
53			return nil, err
54		}
55		p.Kind = PushKind(kind)
56		return p, nil
57	}
58	
59	func (r *PushRepo) ListByArticle(ctx context.Context, articleID int64) ([]*Push, error) {
60		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
61			`SELECT `+pushColumns+` FROM pushes WHERE article_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`, articleID,
62		)
63		if err != nil {
64			return nil, err
65		}
66		defer rows.Close()
67		var out []*Push
68		for rows.Next() {
69			p, err := scanPush(rows)
70			if err != nil {
71				return nil, err
72			}
73			out = append(out, p)
74		}
75		return out, rows.Err()
76	}
77	
78	// scoreDelta returns the recommend_score contribution of a push of the
79	// given kind. Positive on add; negate on delete. Centralized so Create
80	// and Delete agree on the algebra.
81	func scoreDelta(kind PushKind) (int, error) {
82		switch kind {
83		case PushKindPush:
84			return 1, nil
85		case PushKindBoo:
86			return -1, nil
87		case PushKindArrow:
88			return 0, nil
89		}
90		return 0, fmt.Errorf("invalid push kind: %q", kind)
91	}
92	
93	// Create inserts a push and updates the cached recommend_score on the
94	// article (push: +1, boo: -1, arrow: 0). Both writes happen under the
95	// store-level writeMu so they're effectively atomic from the app's view;
96	// SQLite's WAL gives us read-consistency for the recompute.
97	func (r *PushRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, articleID, userID int64, userUserID string, kind PushKind, body string) (*Push, error) {
98		delta, err := scoreDelta(kind)
99		if err != nil {
100			return nil, err
101		}
102	
103		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
104		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
105		tx, err := r.s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
106		if err != nil {
107			return nil, err
108		}
109		defer tx.Rollback()
110	
111		res, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
112			`INSERT INTO pushes (article_id, user_id, user_userid, kind, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
113			articleID, userID, userUserID, string(kind), body,
114		)
115		if err != nil {
116			return nil, err
117		}
118		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
119		if err != nil {
120			return nil, err
121		}
122		if delta != 0 {
123			if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
124				`UPDATE articles SET recommend_score = recommend_score + ? WHERE id = ?`,
125				delta, articleID,
126			); err != nil {
127				return nil, err
128			}
129		}
130		if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
131			return nil, err
132		}
133	
134		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+pushColumns+` FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, id)
135		return scanPush(row)
136	}
137	
138	// Delete hard-deletes a push and reverts its score contribution on the
139	// parent article. Permission: requester must be the push author OR have
140	// a role at-or-above mod. Returns ErrPermissionDenied / ErrPushNotFound.
141	// Mirrors Create's writeMu+tx discipline so concurrent deletes can't
142	// double-revert the score.
143	func (r *PushRepo) Delete(ctx context.Context, pushID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role) error {
144		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
145		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
146		tx, err := r.s.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
147		if err != nil {
148			return err
149		}
150		defer tx.Rollback()
151	
152		var authorID, articleID int64
153		var kindStr string
154		err = tx.QueryRowContext(ctx,
155			`SELECT user_id, article_id, kind FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, pushID,
156		).Scan(&authorID, &articleID, &kindStr)
157		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
158			return ErrPushNotFound
159		}
160		if err != nil {
161			return err
162		}
163		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
164			return ErrPermissionDenied
165		}
166		delta, err := scoreDelta(PushKind(kindStr))
167		if err != nil {
168			return err
169		}
170		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM pushes WHERE id = ?`, pushID); err != nil {
171			return err
172		}
173		if delta != 0 {
174			if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx,
175				`UPDATE articles SET recommend_score = recommend_score - ? WHERE id = ?`,
176				delta, articleID,
177			); err != nil {
178				return err
179			}
180		}
181		return tx.Commit()
182	}
183

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"

7		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textarea"

10	)
11	
12	const (
13		composeFocusTitle = iota
14		composeFocusBody
15	)
16	
17	type postComposeModel struct {
18		deps    Deps
19		boardID int64
20		title   textinput.Model
21		body    textarea.Model
22		focus   int
23		width   int
24		height  int
25		err     string
26	}
27	
28	func newPostComposeModel(deps Deps, boardID int64) postComposeModel {
29		ti := textinput.New()
30		ti.Placeholder = "標題 title"
31		ti.CharLimit = 64
32		ti.Width = 60
33		ti.Focus()
34	
35		ta := textarea.New()
36		ta.Placeholder = "內容 body…"
37		ta.CharLimit = 8000
38		ta.SetWidth(72)
39		ta.SetHeight(12)
40	
41		return postComposeModel{
42			deps:    deps,
43			boardID: boardID,
44			title:   ti,
45			body:    ta,
46			focus:   composeFocusTitle,
47		}
48	}
49	
50	func (m postComposeModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
51	
52	func (m postComposeModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
53		switch msg := msg.(type) {
54		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
55			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
56			bodyW := max(40, msg.Width-8)
57			bodyH := max(8, msg.Height-12)
58			m.body.SetWidth(bodyW)
59			m.body.SetHeight(bodyH)
60			m.title.Width = max(20, msg.Width-12)
61			return m, nil
62	
63		case tea.KeyMsg:
64			switch msg.String() {
65			case "esc":
66				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: m.boardID} }
67			case "tab":
68				m.toggleFocus()
69				return m, nil
70			case "ctrl+s":
71				return m.submit()
72			}
73		}
74	
75		var cmd tea.Cmd
76		if m.focus == composeFocusTitle {
77			// In title field, Enter advances to body.
78			if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok && k.String() == "enter" {
79				m.toggleFocus()
80				return m, nil
81			}
82			m.title, cmd = m.title.Update(msg)
83		} else {
84			m.body, cmd = m.body.Update(msg)
85		}
86		return m, cmd
87	}
88	
89	func (m *postComposeModel) toggleFocus() {
90		if m.focus == composeFocusTitle {
91			m.focus = composeFocusBody
92			m.title.Blur()
93			m.body.Focus()
94		} else {
95			m.focus = composeFocusTitle
96			m.body.Blur()
97			m.title.Focus()
98		}
99	}
100	
101	func (m postComposeModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
102		title := strings.TrimSpace(m.title.Value())
103		body := m.body.Value()
104		if title == "" {
105			m.err = "title is required"
106			return m, nil
107		}
108		if strings.TrimSpace(body) == "" {
109			m.err = "body is required"
110			return m, nil
111		}
112		ctx := context.Background()
113		u := m.deps.User
114		art, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Create(ctx, m.boardID, u.ID, u.UserID, title, body)
115		if err != nil {
116			m.err = err.Error()
117			return m, nil
118		}
119		if err := m.deps.Store.Users().IncrementPosts(ctx, u.ID); err != nil {
120			m.err = err.Error()
121			return m, nil
122		}
123		// Broadcast to other live sessions; recipients filter by BoardID and
124		// re-fetch the canonical list (mirrors the PushAddedMsg pattern).
125		if m.deps.Broker != nil {
126			m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, ArticleAddedMsg{
127				BoardID:      m.boardID,
128				ArticleID:    art.ID,
129				AuthorUserID: u.UserID,
130				Title:        art.Title,
131			})
132		}
133		return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: m.boardID} }
134	}
135	
136	func (m postComposeModel) View() string {
137		var b strings.Builder
138		b.WriteString("\n")
139		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 發表新文章 New Post "))
140		b.WriteString("\n\n")
141	
142		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title:") + "\n")
143		b.WriteString("  " + m.title.View() + "\n\n")
144	
145		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Body:") + "\n")
146		b.WriteString("  " + indent(m.body.View(), "  ") + "\n")
147	
148		if m.err != "" {
149			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
150		}
151		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab switch field · Enter (in title) → body · Ctrl+S send · Esc cancel"))
152		b.WriteString("\n")
153		return b.String()
154	}
155	
156	func indent(s, prefix string) string {
157		lines := strings.Split(s, "\n")
158		for i, ln := range lines {
159			if i == 0 {
160				continue
161			}
162			lines[i] = prefix + ln
163		}
164		return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
165	}

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/migrate.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"embed"
7		"fmt"
8		"sort"
9		"strconv"
10		"strings"
11	)
12	
13	//go:embed migrations/*.sql
14	var migrationsFS embed.FS
15	
16	// apply runs every migration whose version number isn't already in
17	// schema_migrations. Filenames must be NNNN_name.sql; the leading integer is
18	// the version. Migrations are applied in ascending version order, each in
19	// its own transaction.
20	func apply(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) error {
21		if _, err := db.ExecContext(ctx, `
22			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_migrations (
23				version    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
24				applied_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
25			)
26		`); err != nil {
27			return fmt.Errorf("create schema_migrations: %w", err)
28		}
29	
30		applied, err := loadApplied(ctx, db)
31		if err != nil {
32			return err
33		}
34	
35		entries, err := migrationsFS.ReadDir("migrations")
36		if err != nil {
37			return fmt.Errorf("read embedded migrations: %w", err)
38		}
39		type migration struct {
40			version  int
41			filename string
42		}
43		var pending []migration
44		for _, e := range entries {
45			if e.IsDir() || !strings.HasSuffix(e.Name(), ".sql") {
46				continue
47			}
48			v, err := parseVersion(e.Name())
49			if err != nil {
50				return err
51			}
52			if !applied[v] {
53				pending = append(pending, migration{version: v, filename: e.Name()})
54			}
55		}
56		sort.Slice(pending, func(i, j int) bool { return pending[i].version < pending[j].version })
57	
58		for _, m := range pending {
59			body, err := migrationsFS.ReadFile("migrations/" + m.filename)
60			if err != nil {
61				return fmt.Errorf("read migration %s: %w", m.filename, err)
62			}
63			if err := runOne(ctx, db, m.version, string(body)); err != nil {
64				return fmt.Errorf("apply %s: %w", m.filename, err)
65			}
66		}
67		return nil
68	}
69	
70	func loadApplied(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) (map[int]bool, error) {
71		rows, err := db.QueryContext(ctx, `SELECT version FROM schema_migrations`)
72		if err != nil {
73			return nil, fmt.Errorf("load applied migrations: %w", err)
74		}
75		defer rows.Close()
76		out := map[int]bool{}
77		for rows.Next() {
78			var v int
79			if err := rows.Scan(&v); err != nil {
80				return nil, err
81			}
82			out[v] = true
83		}
84		return out, rows.Err()
85	}
86	
87	func runOne(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB, version int, body string) error {
88		tx, err := db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
89		if err != nil {
90			return err
91		}
92		defer tx.Rollback()
93		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, body); err != nil {
94			return err
95		}
96		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, `INSERT INTO schema_migrations(version) VALUES (?)`, version); err != nil {
97			return err
98		}
99		return tx.Commit()
100	}
101	
102	func parseVersion(filename string) (int, error) {
103		// "0001_init.sql" -> 1
104		idx := strings.IndexByte(filename, '_')
105		if idx <= 0 {
106			return 0, fmt.Errorf("migration filename missing version prefix: %s", filename)
107		}
108		v, err := strconv.Atoi(filename[:idx])
109		if err != nil {
110			return 0, fmt.Errorf("parse version in %s: %w", filename, err)
111		}
112		return v, nil
113	}
114
```

 `./internal/auth/seed.go`

```text
1	package auth
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"fmt"
7	
8		"golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
11	)
12	
13	const (
14		// GuestPasswordSentinel is stored as the bcrypt hash for the guest
15		// user. It is deliberately not a valid bcrypt prefix, so even if the
16		// SSH password callback ever falls through to bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword
17		// for guest, the comparison fails. The SSH callback short-circuits guest
18		// before reaching VerifyLogin, so this is belt-and-suspenders.
19		GuestPasswordSentinel = "!"
20	
21		// DefaultAdminPassword is the password baked in when the operator
22		// does not pass -admin-password. Combined with must_change_password=1
23		// and the loopback-only restriction in MustChangePasswordRemoteBlocked,
24		// the operator must rotate it locally before the admin account becomes
25		// reachable from the network.
26		DefaultAdminPassword = "admin"
27	)
28	
29	// SeedOpts controls the bootstrap seed.
30	type SeedOpts struct {
31		// AdminPassword overrides DefaultAdminPassword on first seed.
32		// Empty string = use the baked default. Ignored if admin already exists.
33		AdminPassword string
34	}
35	
36	// SeedSystemAccounts creates the bootstrap admin and guest accounts if
37	// they do not exist. Idempotent — calling on a populated DB is a no-op.
38	// Lives in auth (not store) because it owns the bcrypt cost decision.
39	func SeedSystemAccounts(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, opts SeedOpts) error {
40		if err := seedAdmin(ctx, st, opts); err != nil {
41			return fmt.Errorf("seed admin: %w", err)
42		}
43		if err := seedGuest(ctx, st); err != nil {
44			return fmt.Errorf("seed guest: %w", err)
45		}
46		return nil
47	}
48	
49	func seedAdmin(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, opts SeedOpts) error {
50		if _, err := st.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, ReservedUsernameAdmin); err == nil {
51			return nil
52		} else if !errors.Is(err, store.ErrUserNotFound) {
53			return err
54		}
55		pw := opts.AdminPassword
56		if pw == "" {
57			pw = DefaultAdminPassword
58		}
59		hash, err := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(pw), bcryptCost)
60		if err != nil {
61			return err
62		}
63		_, err = st.Users().InsertSystemAccount(
64			ctx, ReservedUsernameAdmin, string(hash), store.RoleAdmin, true,
65		)
66		return err
67	}
68	
69	func seedGuest(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store) error {
70		if _, err := st.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, ReservedUsernameGuest); err == nil {
71			return nil
72		} else if !errors.Is(err, store.ErrUserNotFound) {
73			return err
74		}
75		_, err := st.Users().InsertSystemAccount(
76			ctx, ReservedUsernameGuest, GuestPasswordSentinel, store.RoleGuest, false,
77		)
78		return err
79	}
80

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/store.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"fmt"
7		"sync"
8	
9		_ "modernc.org/sqlite"
10	)
11	
12	// Store wraps the *sql.DB plus a process-level write mutex used for
13	// multi-statement writes that need to be atomic at the application level
14	// (e.g. inserting a push and recomputing the cached recommend_score).
15	// SQLite WAL handles concurrent reads; this mutex avoids SQLITE_BUSY under
16	// modest contention without spreading sql.Tx everywhere.
17	type Store struct {
18		db      *sql.DB
19		writeMu sync.Mutex
20	
21		users          *UserRepo
22		boards         *BoardRepo
23		articles       *ArticleRepo
24		pushes         *PushRepo
25		waterBalloons  *WaterBalloonRepo
26		mail           *MailRepo
27	}
28	
29	func Open(path string) (*Store, error) {
30		dsn := fmt.Sprintf(
31			"file:%s?_pragma=journal_mode(WAL)&_pragma=foreign_keys(on)&_pragma=busy_timeout(5000)",
32			path,
33		)
34		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite", dsn)
35		if err != nil {
36			return nil, fmt.Errorf("open sqlite: %w", err)
37		}
38		if err := db.PingContext(context.Background()); err != nil {
39			_ = db.Close()
40			return nil, fmt.Errorf("ping sqlite: %w", err)
41		}
42		if err := apply(context.Background(), db); err != nil {
43			_ = db.Close()
44			return nil, fmt.Errorf("migrate: %w", err)
45		}
46	
47		s := &Store{db: db}
48		s.users = &UserRepo{s: s}
49		s.boards = &BoardRepo{s: s}
50		s.articles = &ArticleRepo{s: s}
51		s.pushes = &PushRepo{s: s}
52		s.waterBalloons = &WaterBalloonRepo{s: s}
53		s.mail = &MailRepo{s: s}
54		return s, nil
55	}
56	
57	func (s *Store) Close() error { return s.db.Close() }
58	
59	// DB exposes the raw handle for callers that need it (tests, ad-hoc SQL).
60	// Production code should go through repos.
61	func (s *Store) DB() *sql.DB { return s.db }
62	
63	func (s *Store) Users() *UserRepo                 { return s.users }

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/chat/broker.go`

```text
1	// Package chat is the in-memory hub that connects live SSH sessions for
2	// real-time features (water balloons, push broadcasts). It mirrors PTT's
3	// `userinfo_t` shared-memory table conceptually, but as a process-local
4	// map[user_id][]*Session — no SHM, no daemons.
5	package chat
6	
7	import (
8		"sync"
9	
10		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
11	)
12	
13	// Sender is the minimal slice of *tea.Program the broker actually needs.
14	// Decoupling lets tests substitute a recording fake without spinning up a
15	// real bubbletea program.
16	type Sender interface {
17		Send(tea.Msg)
18	}
19	
20	// Session is one live SSH connection. The same user may have multiple
21	// Sessions (PTT calls this "雙開"); the broker keeps them all and
22	// broadcasts to each.
23	type Session struct {
24		UserID    int64
25		UserIDStr string
26		Program   Sender
27	}
28	
29	// OnlineUser is the externally-visible view of a logged-in user.
30	type OnlineUser struct {
31		UserID    int64
32		UserIDStr string
33		Sessions  int
34	}
35	
36	type Broker struct {
37		mu       sync.RWMutex
38		sessions map[int64][]*Session
39	}
40	
41	func NewBroker() *Broker {
42		return &Broker{sessions: make(map[int64][]*Session)}
43	}
44	
45	func (b *Broker) Register(s *Session) {
46		b.mu.Lock()
47		defer b.mu.Unlock()
48		b.sessions[s.UserID] = append(b.sessions[s.UserID], s)
49	}
50	
51	// Unregister removes a single session for the given user (matched by
52	// Sender identity). If it was the last session, the user disappears from
53	// the online list.
54	func (b *Broker) Unregister(userID int64, prog Sender) {
55		b.mu.Lock()
56		defer b.mu.Unlock()
57		list := b.sessions[userID]
58		for i, s := range list {
59			if s.Program == prog {
60				b.sessions[userID] = append(list[:i], list[i+1:]...)
61				break
62			}
63		}
64		if len(b.sessions[userID]) == 0 {
65			delete(b.sessions, userID)
66		}
67	}
68	
69	// Send delivers msg to every live session of toUID. Returns true if at
70	// least one session received it (i.e. the recipient is online).
71	func (b *Broker) Send(toUID int64, msg tea.Msg) bool {
72		b.mu.RLock()
73		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
74		list := b.sessions[toUID]
75		for _, s := range list {
76			s.Program.Send(msg)
77		}
78		return len(list) > 0
79	}
80	
81	// SendToAll broadcasts to every online session. excludeUID lets the
82	// originator skip themselves (pass 0 to broadcast to everyone).
83	func (b *Broker) SendToAll(excludeUID int64, msg tea.Msg) {
84		b.mu.RLock()
85		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
86		for uid, list := range b.sessions {
87			if uid == excludeUID {
88				continue
89			}
90			for _, s := range list {
91				s.Program.Send(msg)
92			}
93		}
94	}
95	
96	// LookupByUserID returns the numeric user_id for a logged-in account name,
97	// or 0 if nobody by that name is currently online. Case-insensitive.
98	func (b *Broker) LookupByUserID(name string) int64 {
99		b.mu.RLock()
100		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
101		for uid, list := range b.sessions {
102			for _, s := range list {
103				if equalFold(s.UserIDStr, name) {
104					return uid
105				}
106			}
107		}
108		return 0
109	}
110	
111	func (b *Broker) OnlineList() []OnlineUser {
112		b.mu.RLock()
113		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
114		out := make([]OnlineUser, 0, len(b.sessions))
115		for uid, list := range b.sessions {
116			if len(list) == 0 {
117				continue
118			}
119			out = append(out, OnlineUser{
120				UserID:    uid,
121				UserIDStr: list[0].UserIDStr,
122				Sessions:  len(list),
123			})
124		}
125		return out
126	}
127	
128	// SessionsSnapshot returns a defensive copy of every session pointer for
129	// graceful shutdown (so the caller can iterate without holding the lock
130	// while blocking on per-program quits).
131	func (b *Broker) SessionsSnapshot() []*Session {
132		b.mu.RLock()
133		defer b.mu.RUnlock()
134		var out []*Session
135		for _, list := range b.sessions {
136			out = append(out, list...)
137		}
138		return out
139	}
140	
141	// equalFold is strings.EqualFold inlined to avoid the import in this tiny package.
142	func equalFold(a, b string) bool {
143		if len(a) != len(b) {
144			return false
145		}
146		for i := 0; i < len(a); i++ {
147			ca, cb := a[i], b[i]
148			if ca >= 'A' && ca <= 'Z' {
149				ca += 32
150			}
151			if cb >= 'A' && cb <= 'Z' {
152				cb += 32
153			}
154			if ca != cb {
155				return false
156			}
157		}
158		return true
159	}
160
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/auth/auth.go`

```text
1	package auth
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"fmt"
7		"regexp"
8		"strings"
9	
10		"golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
13	)
14	
15	// Reserved SSH usernames. `new` triggers the in-TUI register flow;
16	// `guest` short-circuits to a read-only spectator account; `admin` is
17	// the bootstrapped administrator and may not be re-registered.
18	const (
19		ReservedUsernameNew   = "new"
20		ReservedUsernameGuest = "guest"
21		ReservedUsernameAdmin = "admin"
22	)
23	
24	// IsReservedUsername reports whether s matches one of the three reserved
25	// SSH usernames (case-insensitive). Used by Register to refuse the names
26	// and by the SSH password callback to dispatch to the right flow.
27	func IsReservedUsername(s string) bool {
28		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
29		return strings.EqualFold(s, ReservedUsernameNew) ||
30			strings.EqualFold(s, ReservedUsernameGuest) ||
31			strings.EqualFold(s, ReservedUsernameAdmin)
32	}
33	
34	const (
35		bcryptCost     = 12
36		minPasswordLen = 6
37		maxPasswordLen = 128
38		maxNickname    = 32
39		maxEmail       = 128
40	)
41	
42	var (
43		ErrUserExists       = errors.New("user already exists")
44		ErrInvalidUserID    = errors.New("user id must be 3-12 chars, start with a letter, and use only letters/digits/underscore")
45		ErrInvalidPassword  = fmt.Errorf("password must be %d-%d characters", minPasswordLen, maxPasswordLen)
46		ErrNicknameTooLong  = fmt.Errorf("nickname must be %d characters or fewer", maxNickname)
47		ErrEmailTooLong     = fmt.Errorf("email must be %d characters or fewer", maxEmail)
48		ErrReservedUsername = fmt.Errorf("user id is reserved (%q / %q / %q)",
49			ReservedUsernameNew, ReservedUsernameGuest, ReservedUsernameAdmin)
50		ErrBadCredentials   = errors.New("invalid user id or password")
51	)
52	
53	var userIDRe = regexp.MustCompile(`^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_]{2,11}$`)
54	
55	// ValidatePassword enforces the length rules. Used by Register and the
56	// password-change screen so the rules don't drift between call sites.
57	func ValidatePassword(password string) error {
58		if l := len(password); l < minPasswordLen || l > maxPasswordLen {
59			return ErrInvalidPassword
60		}
61		return nil
62	}
63	
64	// HashPassword returns a bcrypt hash at the package's standard cost.
65	// Callers outside auth (TUI, seed) use this so bcryptCost stays in one place.
66	func HashPassword(password string) (string, error) {
67		hash, err := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(password), bcryptCost)
68		if err != nil {
69			return "", fmt.Errorf("hash password: %w", err)
70		}
71		return string(hash), nil
72	}
73	
74	// VerifyPasswordHash returns nil when the hash matches the password.
75	// Callers outside auth use this instead of importing bcrypt directly.
76	func VerifyPasswordHash(hash, password string) error {
77		return bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte(hash), []byte(password))
78	}
79	
80	// Register validates inputs, hashes the password with bcrypt, and inserts a
81	// new user. Returns ErrUserExists on UNIQUE conflict.
82	func Register(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, userID, password, nickname, email string) (*store.User, error) {
83		userID = strings.TrimSpace(userID)
84		nickname = strings.TrimSpace(nickname)
85		email = strings.TrimSpace(email)
86	
87		if !userIDRe.MatchString(userID) {
88			return nil, ErrInvalidUserID
89		}
90		if IsReservedUsername(userID) {
91			return nil, ErrReservedUsername
92		}
93		if err := ValidatePassword(password); err != nil {
94			return nil, err
95		}
96		if len([]rune(nickname)) > maxNickname {
97			return nil, ErrNicknameTooLong
98		}
99		if len(email) > maxEmail {
100			return nil, ErrEmailTooLong
101		}
102	
103		hash, err := HashPassword(password)
104		if err != nil {
105			return nil, err
106		}
107	
108		u, err := st.Users().Create(ctx, userID, hash, nickname, email)
109		if err != nil {
110			// modernc.org/sqlite returns a wrapped UNIQUE error; check the text.
111			if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "UNIQUE") {
112				return nil, ErrUserExists
113			}
114			return nil, err
115		}
116		return u, nil
117	}
118	
119	// VerifyLogin returns the user record if the password matches, ErrBadCredentials otherwise.
120	// On success, NoteLogin is called to update last_login_at / last_host / num_logins.
121	func VerifyLogin(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, userID, password, host string) (*store.User, error) {
122		u, err := st.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, strings.TrimSpace(userID))
123		if err != nil {
124			if errors.Is(err, store.ErrUserNotFound) {
125				// Run a dummy bcrypt check anyway to even out timing.
126				_ = bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte("$2a$12$invalidinvalidinvalidinvalidinvalidinvalidinvalidinvali"), []byte(password))
127				return nil, ErrBadCredentials
128			}
129			return nil, err
130		}
131		if err := bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte(u.PasswordHash), []byte(password)); err != nil {
132			return nil, ErrBadCredentials
133		}
134		// Skip NoteLogin for guest — the row is shared across every spectator
135		// session, so num_logins / last_login_at are meaningless and the write
136		// would just create contention. Defensive: the SSH password callback
137		// short-circuits guest before calling VerifyLogin in normal flow.
138		if u.Role == store.RoleGuest {
139			return u, nil
140		}
141		if err := st.Users().NoteLogin(ctx, u.ID, host); err != nil {
142			return nil, fmt.Errorf("note login: %w", err)
143		}
144		// Re-fetch so callers see the updated last_login_at / num_logins.
145		return st.Users().GetByID(ctx, u.ID)
146	}
147
```

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
9		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
12	)
13	
14	type articleViewModel struct {
15		deps    Deps
16		article *store.Article
17		pushes  []*store.Push
18		width   int
19		height  int
20		scroll  int
21		loadErr error
22	
23		// push input state
24		pushing  bool
25		pushKind store.PushKind
26		pushIn   textinput.Model
27		err      string
28	
29		// delete state. pendingDelete: showing the y/N confirm overlay.
30		// pushCursor: -1 = no push selected (D targets article); 0..len-1 = D targets that push.
31		pendingDelete bool
32		pushCursor    int
33	}
34	
35	func newArticleViewModel(deps Deps, articleID int64) articleViewModel {
36		ctx := context.Background()
37		a, err := deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, articleID)
38		if err != nil {
39			return articleViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
40		}
41		pushes, err := deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, articleID)
42	
43		ti := textinput.New()
44		ti.Placeholder = "comment…"
45		ti.CharLimit = 80
46		ti.Width = 60
47	
48		return articleViewModel{deps: deps, article: a, pushes: pushes, loadErr: err, pushIn: ti, pushCursor: -1}
49	}
50	
51	// canDeleteArticle returns true if the current user may delete the loaded
52	// article (author, or mod-or-above). Used both as a permission gate at
53	// the key handler and to decide whether to render the help-line hint.
54	func (m articleViewModel) canDeleteArticle() bool {
55		if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
56			return false
57		}
58		if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
59			return true
60		}
61		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
62	}
63	
64	// canDeletePush mirrors canDeleteArticle for the push at index idx.
65	func (m articleViewModel) canDeletePush(idx int) bool {
66		if m.deps.User == nil || idx < 0 || idx >= len(m.pushes) {
67			return false
68		}
69		if m.deps.User.ID == m.pushes[idx].UserID {
70			return true
71		}
72		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
73	}
74	
75	// canPush returns true if the current user is allowed to add new pushes.
76	// Guests can read but not write.
77	func (m articleViewModel) canPush() bool {
78		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest
79	}
80	
81	func (m articleViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
82	
83	func (m articleViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
84		switch msg := msg.(type) {
85		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
86			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
87			return m, nil
88	
89		case PushAddedMsg:
90			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
91				ctx := context.Background()
92				// Re-fetch from DB so timestamps and recommend_score reflect
93				// the canonical state, not the just-synthesized broker payload.
94				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
95					m.pushes = pushes
96				}
97				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
98					m.article = a
99				}
100			}
```

230					m.pendingDelete = false

232				}
233				// Refresh the push list and re-fetch the article so its

261	
262	// bodyLineCount returns the number of lines View will render for the
263	// loaded article (matches strings.Split(body, "\n") used in View).
264	func (m articleViewModel) bodyLineCount() int {
265		if m.article == nil {
266			return 0
267		}
268		return len(strings.Split(m.article.Body, "\n"))
269	}
270	
271	// viewportLines mirrors the maxLines computation in View so g/G/PgDn
272	// agree on how many lines fit on screen.
273	func (m articleViewModel) viewportLines() int {
274		return max(m.height-16, 5)
275	}
276	
277	func (m articleViewModel) openPush(k store.PushKind) tea.Model {
278		m.pushing = true
279		m.pushKind = k
280		m.pushIn.SetValue("")
281		m.pushIn.Focus()
282		m.err = ""
283		return m
284	}
285	
286	func (m articleViewModel) updatePushInput(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
287		switch msg.String() {
288		case "esc":
289			m.pushing = false
290			m.pushIn.Blur()
291			return m, nil
292		case "enter":
293			body := strings.TrimSpace(m.pushIn.Value())
294			if body == "" && m.pushKind != store.PushKindArrow {
295				m.err = "comment required for 推/噓"
296				return m, nil
297			}
298			ctx := context.Background()
299			u := m.deps.User
300			p, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().Create(ctx, m.article.ID, u.ID, u.UserID, m.pushKind, body)
301			if err != nil {
302				m.err = err.Error()
303				return m, nil
304			}
305			// Append locally so the originator sees it immediately.
306			m.pushes = append(m.pushes, p)
307			if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
308				m.article = a
309			}
310			m.pushing = false
311			m.pushIn.Blur()
312			// Broadcast to other live sessions; they filter by ArticleID.
313			if m.deps.Broker != nil {
314				m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, PushAddedMsg{
315					ArticleID:  m.article.ID,
316					UserUserID: u.UserID,
317					Kind:       string(m.pushKind),
318					Body:       body,
319				})
320			}
321			return m, nil
322		}
323		var cmd tea.Cmd
324		m.pushIn, cmd = m.pushIn.Update(msg)
325		return m, cmd
326	}
327	
328	func (m articleViewModel) View() string {
329		if m.loadErr != nil {
330			return "\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n"
331		}
332		if m.article == nil {
333			return "\n  " + StyleDim.Render("(no article)") + "\n"
334		}
335		a := m.article
336	
337		var b strings.Builder
338		b.WriteString("\n")
339		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 文章 #%d · %s ", a.ID, a.AuthorUserID)))
340		b.WriteString("\n\n")
341	
342		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title: ") + a.Title + "\n")
343		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Date:  ") + a.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05") + "\n")
344		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Score: ") + fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore) + "\n")
345		b.WriteString("\n")
346	
347		bodyLines := strings.Split(a.Body, "\n")
348		start := min(m.scroll, len(bodyLines))
349		maxLines := max(m.height-16, 5)
350		end := min(start+maxLines, len(bodyLines))
351		for _, line := range bodyLines[start:end] {
352			b.WriteString("  " + line + "\n")
353		}
354		if start > 0 || end < len(bodyLines) {
355			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("[lines %d-%d / %d]", start+1, end, len(bodyLines))))
356			b.WriteString("\n")
357		}
358	
359		if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
360			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("── 推文 (%d) ──", len(m.pushes))) + "\n")
361			for i, p := range m.pushes {
362				ts := p.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04")
363				gutter := "  "
364				if i == m.pushCursor {
365					gutter = "▸ "
366				}
367				line := fmt.Sprintf("%s%s %s %s  %s",
368					gutter,
369					renderPushKind(p.Kind),
370					PadRight(p.UserUserID, 14),
371					p.Body,
372					StyleDim.Render(ts),
373				)
374				if i == m.pushCursor {
375					line = StyleHighlight.Render(line)
376				}
377				b.WriteString(line + "\n")
378			}
379		}
380	
381		if m.pushing {
382			b.WriteString("\n  ")
383			b.WriteString(renderPushKind(m.pushKind) + " ")
384			b.WriteString(m.pushIn.View())
385			b.WriteString("\n")
386			if m.err != "" {
387				b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
388			}
389			b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("Enter send · Esc cancel"))
390		} else if m.pendingDelete {
391			prompt := "確定刪除這篇文章? (y/N)"
392			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.pushCursor < len(m.pushes) {
393				prompt = fmt.Sprintf("確定刪除推文 #%d? (y/N)", m.pushCursor+1)
394			}
395			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+prompt))
396		} else {
397			help := "j/k scroll"
398			if m.canPush() {
399				help += " · + 推 · - 噓 · = →"
400			}
401			if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
402				help += " · p/P 選推文"
403			}
404			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.canDeletePush(m.pushCursor) {
405				help += " · D 刪除推文"
406			} else if m.canDeleteArticle() {
407				help += " · D 刪除文章"
408			}
409			help += " · Esc/← back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
410			if m.err != "" {
411				b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err))
412			}
413			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
414		}
415		b.WriteString("\n")
416		return b.String()
417	}
418	
419	func renderPushKind(k store.PushKind) string {
420		switch k {
421		case store.PushKindPush:
422			return StylePushKind.Render("推")
423		case store.PushKindBoo:
424			return StyleBooKind.Render("噓")
425		case store.PushKindArrow:
426			return StyleArrowKind.Render("→")
427		}
428		return "?"
429	}

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type User struct {
11		ID                 int64
12		UserID             string
13		PasswordHash       string
14		Nickname           string
15		RealName           string
16		Email              string
17		NumLogins          int64
18		NumPosts           int64
19		LastLoginAt        sql.NullTime
20		LastHost           sql.NullString
21		CreatedAt          time.Time
22		Role               Role
23		MustChangePassword bool
24	}
25	
26	type UserRepo struct{ s *Store }
27	
28	var (
29		ErrUserNotFound = errors.New("user not found")
30		ErrInvalidRole  = errors.New("invalid role")
31	)
32	
33	const userColumns = `id, user_id, password_hash, nickname, realname, email,
34		num_logins, num_posts, last_login_at, last_host, created_at, role, must_change_password`
35	
36	func scanUser(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*User, error) {
37		u := &User{}
38		var mustChange int64
39		err := row.Scan(
40			&u.ID, &u.UserID, &u.PasswordHash, &u.Nickname, &u.RealName, &u.Email,
41			&u.NumLogins, &u.NumPosts, &u.LastLoginAt, &u.LastHost, &u.CreatedAt,
42			&u.Role, &mustChange,
43		)
44		u.MustChangePassword = mustChange != 0
45		return u, err
46	}
47	
48	func (r *UserRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, userID, passwordHash, nickname, email string) (*User, error) {
49		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
50		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
51		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
52			`INSERT INTO users (user_id, password_hash, nickname, email) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
53			userID, passwordHash, nickname, email,
54		)
55		if err != nil {
56			return nil, err
57		}
58		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
59		if err != nil {
60			return nil, err
61		}
62		return r.GetByID(ctx, id)
63	}
64	
65	func (r *UserRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*User, error) {
66		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users WHERE id = ?`, id)
67		u, err := scanUser(row)
68		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
69			return nil, ErrUserNotFound
70		}
71		return u, err
72	}
73	
74	func (r *UserRepo) GetByUserID(ctx context.Context, userID string) (*User, error) {
75		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users WHERE user_id = ? COLLATE NOCASE`, userID)
76		u, err := scanUser(row)
77		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
78			return nil, ErrUserNotFound
79		}
80		return u, err
81	}
82	
83	func (r *UserRepo) NoteLogin(ctx context.Context, id int64, host string) error {
84		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
85		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
86		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
87			`UPDATE users SET last_login_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, last_host = ?, num_logins = num_logins + 1 WHERE id = ?`,
88			host, id,
89		)
90		return err
91	}
92	
93	func (r *UserRepo) IncrementPosts(ctx context.Context, id int64) error {
94		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
95		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
96		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
97			`UPDATE users SET num_posts = num_posts + 1 WHERE id = ?`, id,
98		)
99		return err
100	}
101	
102	// InsertSystemAccount creates a privileged user (admin or guest) with the
103	// given role and must_change_password flag, bypassing the regular Create
104	// path which always defaults role='user'. Used only by the bootstrap seed.
105	func (r *UserRepo) InsertSystemAccount(ctx context.Context, userID, passwordHash string, role Role, mustChangePassword bool) (*User, error) {
106		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
107		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
108		mc := int64(0)
109		if mustChangePassword {
110			mc = 1
111		}
112		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
113			`INSERT INTO users (user_id, password_hash, role, must_change_password) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
114			userID, passwordHash, string(role), mc,
115		)
116		if err != nil {
117			return nil, err
118		}
119		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
120		if err != nil {
121			return nil, err
122		}
123		return r.GetByID(ctx, id)
124	}
125	
126	// SetPassword updates the password hash and clears must_change_password
127	// in one statement. The TUI password-change screen calls this after
128	// validating the current password and the new-password rules.
129	func (r *UserRepo) SetPassword(ctx context.Context, id int64, passwordHash string) error {
130		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
131		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
132		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
133			`UPDATE users SET password_hash = ?, must_change_password = 0 WHERE id = ?`,
134			passwordHash, id,
135		)
136		return err
137	}
138	
139	// SetRole updates the role of the given user. Callers (admin user-management
140	// screen) are responsible for enforcing higher-level invariants like the
141	// last-admin guard via CountByRole.
142	func (r *UserRepo) SetRole(ctx context.Context, id int64, role Role) error {
143		if !role.Valid() {
144			return ErrInvalidRole
145		}
146		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
147		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
148		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
149			`UPDATE users SET role = ? WHERE id = ?`, string(role), id,
150		)
151		return err
152	}
153	
154	// ListAll returns users ordered by id, paginated. limit<=0 defaults to 20.
155	func (r *UserRepo) ListAll(ctx context.Context, limit, offset int) ([]*User, error) {
156		if limit <= 0 {
157			limit = 20
158		}
159		if offset < 0 {
160			offset = 0
161		}
162		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
163			`SELECT `+userColumns+` FROM users ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT ? OFFSET ?`,
164			limit, offset,
165		)
166		if err != nil {
167			return nil, err
168		}
169		defer rows.Close()
170		var out []*User
171		for rows.Next() {
172			u, err := scanUser(rows)
173			if err != nil {
174				return nil, err
175			}
176			out = append(out, u)
177		}
178		return out, rows.Err()
179	}
180	
181	// CountByRole returns the number of users currently assigned a given role.
182	// Used by the admin screen's last-admin guard.
183	func (r *UserRepo) CountByRole(ctx context.Context, role Role) (int, error) {
184		var n int
185		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
186			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users WHERE role = ?`, string(role),
187		).Scan(&n)
188		return n, err
189	}
190

13	func TestArticles_CreateAndGet(t *testing.T) {
14		ctx := context.Background()
15		st := storetest.New(t)
16		user := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "Alice")
17		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
18	
19		got, err := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "標題", "內容\n第二行")
20		if err != nil {
21			t.Fatalf("Create: %v", err)
22		}
23		if got.ID == 0 {
24			t.Error("Create returned zero ID")
25		}
26		if got.Title != "標題" || got.Body != "內容\n第二行" {
27			t.Errorf("title/body mismatch: %q / %q", got.Title, got.Body)
28		}
29		if got.RecommendScore != 0 {
30			t.Errorf("RecommendScore = %d, want 0", got.RecommendScore)
31		}
32	
33		fetched, err := st.Articles().GetByID(ctx, got.ID)
34		if err != nil {
35			t.Fatalf("GetByID: %v", err)
36		}
37		if fetched.AuthorUserID != "alice" {
38			t.Errorf("AuthorUserID = %q, want alice", fetched.AuthorUserID)
39		}

122		}

128		}
129	
130		// Wrong board: 0/0 even though the article exists elsewhere.
131		prev, next, _ = st.Articles().NeighboursOf(ctx, other.ID, b.ID)
132		if prev != 0 || next != 0 {
133			t.Errorf("wrong board: prev=%d next=%d, want 0/0", prev, next)
134		}

137		d, _ := st.Articles().Create(ctx, other.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "alone", "")
138		prev, next, _ = st.Articles().NeighboursOf(ctx, other.ID, d.ID)

-- Track edits to articles. NULL = never edited; the original CreatedAt is the
-- only authoritative timestamp. The article-edit screen and ArticleRepo.Update
-- set this to CURRENT_TIMESTAMP on every successful update.
--
-- Ordering: ListByBoard intentionally still sorts by created_at, so an edited
-- article does NOT bubble up to the top — bookmark/next-prev semantics stay
-- intact.

ALTER TABLE articles ADD COLUMN updated_at DATETIME;

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```go
// Package markdown is the round-trip serializer/parser for articles.
//
// An article (with optional pushes) renders to a self-describing
// human-readable document with YAML-ish frontmatter:
//
//	---
//	title: 歡迎來到 SSH-BBS
//	board: Welcome
//	author: admin
//	created_at: 2026-04-30T12:34:56Z
//	updated_at: 2026-04-30T13:00:00Z
//	score: 0
//	id: 1
//	---
//
//	[arbitrary body — markdown or plain text]
//
//	<!-- sshbbs:pushes -->
//	- 推 [alice] 2026-04-30T12:35:01Z  great post
//	- 噓 [bob]   2026-04-30T12:35:14Z  disagree
//	- → [carol]  2026-04-30T12:35:20Z  fyi
//
// The format is intentionally narrow: each frontmatter line is
// `key: value`, no nesting, no quoting. Push lines are
// `- <glyph> [<author>] <RFC3339-ts>  <body>` with a TWO-space separator
// between timestamp and body.
//
// The package is a leaf — pure functions over store types, no DB access.
package markdown

import (
	"fmt"
	"strconv"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
)

const (
	frontmatterDelim = "---"

	// PushesSentinel marks the start of the pushes block. Public so callers
	// (UI, tests, docs) can warn users not to embed this exact string in
	// their article body. If a body contains the sentinel, Format will
	// suffix it with the literal-marker so Parse can restore the original.
	PushesSentinel = "<!-- sshbbs:pushes -->"

	// pushesLiteralMarker is appended to a sentinel that legitimately
	// appears inside the body. Parse strips it back out.
	pushesLiteralMarker = "<!-- sshbbs:literal -->"
)

// FormatOpts controls the output of Format.
type FormatOpts struct {
	// IncludePushes appends the pushes section after the body.
	IncludePushes bool
	// BoardName is rendered into the frontmatter. Pass the board name
	// (the package can't look it up — store types don't carry it).
	BoardName string
}

// Parsed is the round-trip target. It deliberately does NOT return a
// *store.Article because Parse runs without DB context: the caller must
// resolve BoardName → BoardID and AuthorName → AuthorID before INSERT.
type Parsed struct {
	Title      string
	BoardName  string
	AuthorName string
	CreatedAt  time.Time
	UpdatedAt  time.Time
	ScoreHint  int64
	IDHint     int64
	Body       string
	Pushes     []ParsedPush
	// Extra preserves any frontmatter key we don't recognize, so future
	// schema additions don't drop data on a round-trip through an older
	// build.
	Extra map[string]string
}

// ParsedPush mirrors store.Push but with author as a name (not user_id) and
// without a DB-side ID; the caller decides whether/how to materialize it.
type ParsedPush struct {
	Kind      store.PushKind
	Author    string
	CreatedAt time.Time
	Body      string
}

// Format renders an article (plus optional pushes) to the canonical text
// form. Returns an error only on a nil article; otherwise it always
// produces a valid, parseable document.
func Format(a *store.Article, pushes []*store.Push, opts FormatOpts) (string, error) {
	if a == nil {
		return "", fmt.Errorf("markdown.Format: article is nil")
	}
	var b strings.Builder
	b.WriteString(frontmatterDelim + "\n")
	writeFrontmatter(&b, "title", sanitizeFM(a.Title))
	if opts.BoardName != "" {
		writeFrontmatter(&b, "board", sanitizeFM(opts.BoardName))
	}
	if a.AuthorUserID != "" {
		writeFrontmatter(&b, "author", sanitizeFM(a.AuthorUserID))
	}
	if !a.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
		writeFrontmatter(&b, "created_at", a.CreatedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339))
	}
	if a.UpdatedAt.Valid {
		writeFrontmatter(&b, "updated_at", a.UpdatedAt.Time.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339))
	}
	writeFrontmatter(&b, "score", strconv.FormatInt(a.RecommendScore, 10))
	if a.ID > 0 {
		writeFrontmatter(&b, "id", strconv.FormatInt(a.ID, 10))
	}
	b.WriteString(frontmatterDelim + "\n\n")

	body := a.Body
	// Disambiguate any literal sentinel inside the body so Parse won't
	// mis-cleave at it. Best-effort: if the user already has the
	// literal-marker after the sentinel, we leave it alone.
	if strings.Contains(body, PushesSentinel) {
		body = strings.ReplaceAll(body, PushesSentinel+pushesLiteralMarker, "\x00LITERAL_MARKED\x00")
		body = strings.ReplaceAll(body, PushesSentinel, PushesSentinel+pushesLiteralMarker)
		body = strings.ReplaceAll(body, "\x00LITERAL_MARKED\x00", PushesSentinel+pushesLiteralMarker)
	}
	b.WriteString(body)
	if !strings.HasSuffix(body, "\n") {
		b.WriteString("\n")
	}

	if opts.IncludePushes && len(pushes) > 0 {
		b.WriteString("\n" + PushesSentinel + "\n")
		for _, p := range pushes {
			ts := p.CreatedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
			// Push body is single-line by DB convention; flatten just in case.
			pb := strings.ReplaceAll(p.Body, "\n", " ")
			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "- %s [%s] %s  %s\n", p.Kind.Glyph(), p.UserUserID, ts, pb)
		}
	}

	return b.String(), nil
}

// Parse reads markdown back into a Parsed. Permissive: missing frontmatter
// or pushes section degrade to zero values; the whole input becomes Body
// when no frontmatter is present.
func Parse(md string) (*Parsed, error) {
	p := &Parsed{Extra: map[string]string{}}
	rest := md

	if strings.HasPrefix(rest, frontmatterDelim+"\n") {
		rest = rest[len(frontmatterDelim)+1:]
		closeIdx := strings.Index(rest, "\n"+frontmatterDelim+"\n")
		closeAtEOF := false
		if closeIdx < 0 && strings.HasSuffix(rest, "\n"+frontmatterDelim) {
			closeIdx = len(rest) - len("\n"+frontmatterDelim)
			closeAtEOF = true
		}
		if closeIdx >= 0 {
			fm := rest[:closeIdx]
			if closeAtEOF {
				rest = ""
			} else {
				rest = rest[closeIdx+len("\n"+frontmatterDelim+"\n"):]
			}
			parseFrontmatter(p, fm)
		}
	}

	rest = strings.TrimLeft(rest, "\n")
	if idx := strings.Index(rest, PushesSentinel); idx >= 0 {
		// The sentinel may be followed by literal-marker, which means it
		// belongs to the body. Walk past every literal-marked occurrence.
		head := rest
		offset := 0
		for {
			i := strings.Index(head, PushesSentinel)
			if i < 0 {
				// Fell through without a real sentinel — entire rest is body.
				p.Body = stripLiteralMarkers(strings.TrimRight(rest, "\n"))
				return p, nil
			}
			afterSentinel := i + len(PushesSentinel)
			if strings.HasPrefix(head[afterSentinel:], pushesLiteralMarker) {
				// Skip — it's part of the body. Continue searching.
				next := afterSentinel + len(pushesLiteralMarker)
				offset += next
				head = head[next:]
				continue
			}
			// Real sentinel — split.
			cut := offset + i
			body := strings.TrimRight(rest[:cut], "\n")
			p.Body = stripLiteralMarkers(body)
			parsePushes(p, rest[cut+len(PushesSentinel):])
			return p, nil
		}
	}
	p.Body = stripLiteralMarkers(strings.TrimRight(rest, "\n"))
	return p, nil
}

func writeFrontmatter(b *strings.Builder, key, val string) {
	b.WriteString(key)
	b.WriteString(": ")
	b.WriteString(val)
	b.WriteString("\n")
}

func sanitizeFM(s string) string {
	// Frontmatter values are single-line. CR/LF would break the parser;
	// flatten to space.
	s = strings.ReplaceAll(s, "\r", " ")
	s = strings.ReplaceAll(s, "\n", " ")
	return s
}

func parseFrontmatter(p *Parsed, fm string) {
	for _, line := range strings.Split(fm, "\n") {
		line = strings.TrimRight(line, "\r")
		if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
			continue
		}
		idx := strings.IndexByte(line, ':')
		if idx <= 0 {
			continue
		}
		key := strings.TrimSpace(line[:idx])
		val := strings.TrimSpace(line[idx+1:])
		switch key {
		case "title":
			p.Title = val
		case "board":
			p.BoardName = val
		case "author":
			p.AuthorName = val
		case "created_at":
			if t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, val); err == nil {
				p.CreatedAt = t
			}
		case "updated_at":
			if t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, val); err == nil {
				p.UpdatedAt = t
			}
		case "score":
			if n, err := strconv.ParseInt(val, 10, 64); err == nil {
				p.ScoreHint = n
			}
		case "id":
			if n, err := strconv.ParseInt(val, 10, 64); err == nil {
				p.IDHint = n
			}
		default:
			p.Extra[key] = val
		}
	}
}

func parsePushes(p *Parsed, block string) {
	for _, line := range strings.Split(block, "\n") {
		line = strings.TrimRight(line, "\r")
		line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
		if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "- ") {
			continue
		}
		if push, ok := parsePushLine(line[2:]); ok {
			p.Pushes = append(p.Pushes, push)
		}
	}
}

func parsePushLine(s string) (ParsedPush, bool) {
	// Format: "<glyph> [<author>] <RFC3339-ts>  <body>"
	s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
	if s == "" {
		return ParsedPush{}, false
	}

	// Glyph: take everything before the first whitespace.
	wsIdx := strings.IndexAny(s, " \t")
	if wsIdx <= 0 {
		return ParsedPush{}, false
	}
	glyph := s[:wsIdx]
	rest := strings.TrimLeft(s[wsIdx:], " \t")

	var kind store.PushKind
	switch glyph {
	case "推":
		kind = store.PushKindPush
	case "噓":
		kind = store.PushKindBoo
	case "→":
		kind = store.PushKindArrow
	default:
		return ParsedPush{}, false
	}

	if !strings.HasPrefix(rest, "[") {
		return ParsedPush{}, false
	}
	closeBracket := strings.IndexByte(rest, ']')
	if closeBracket <= 1 {
		return ParsedPush{}, false
	}
	author := rest[1:closeBracket]
	rest = strings.TrimLeft(rest[closeBracket+1:], " \t")

	// Timestamp + body separated by 2 spaces. If absent, fall back to one
	// space (lossy but tolerant of hand-edited files).
	var tsStr, body string
	if sepIdx := strings.Index(rest, "  "); sepIdx >= 0 {
		tsStr = rest[:sepIdx]
		body = strings.TrimLeft(rest[sepIdx:], " \t")
	} else if spaceIdx := strings.IndexAny(rest, " \t"); spaceIdx >= 0 {
		tsStr = rest[:spaceIdx]
		body = strings.TrimLeft(rest[spaceIdx:], " \t")
	} else {
		tsStr = rest
		body = ""
	}

	var ts time.Time
	if tsStr != "" {
		if t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, tsStr); err == nil {
			ts = t
		}
	}
	return ParsedPush{Kind: kind, Author: author, CreatedAt: ts, Body: body}, true
}

func stripLiteralMarkers(s string) string {
	return strings.ReplaceAll(s, PushesSentinel+pushesLiteralMarker, PushesSentinel)
}

```

package markdown_test

import (
	"database/sql"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/markdown"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
)

func mustParseTime(t *testing.T, s string) time.Time {
	t.Helper()
	v, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, s)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("parse %q: %v", s, err)
	}
	return v
}

func sampleArticle() *store.Article {
	return &store.Article{
		ID:             42,
		BoardID:        7,
		AuthorID:       3,
		AuthorUserID:   "alice",
		Title:          "Hello, BBS",
		Body:           "first line\nsecond line\n",
		RecommendScore: 5,
		CreatedAt:      time.Date(2026, 4, 30, 12, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC),
		UpdatedAt:      sql.NullTime{Time: time.Date(2026, 4, 30, 13, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC), Valid: true},
	}
}

func samplePushes() []*store.Push {
	return []*store.Push{
		{
			ArticleID:  42,
			UserUserID: "bob",
			Kind:       store.PushKindPush,
			Body:       "great",
			CreatedAt:  time.Date(2026, 4, 30, 12, 5, 0, 0, time.UTC),
		},
		{
			ArticleID:  42,
			UserUserID: "carol",
			Kind:       store.PushKindBoo,
			Body:       "meh",
			CreatedAt:  time.Date(2026, 4, 30, 12, 6, 0, 0, time.UTC),
		},
		{
			ArticleID:  42,
			UserUserID: "dave",
			Kind:       store.PushKindArrow,
			Body:       "fyi just looking",
			CreatedAt:  time.Date(2026, 4, 30, 12, 7, 0, 0, time.UTC),
		},
	}
}

func TestFormat_RoundTrip_BodyOnly(t *testing.T) {
	a := sampleArticle()
	out, err := markdown.Format(a, nil, markdown.FormatOpts{BoardName: "Welcome"})
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Format: %v", err)
	}
	if !strings.HasPrefix(out, "---\n") {
		t.Fatalf("missing frontmatter delimiter:\n%s", out)
	}
	if !strings.Contains(out, "title: Hello, BBS\n") {
		t.Errorf("missing title line in:\n%s", out)
	}
	if !strings.Contains(out, "board: Welcome\n") {
		t.Errorf("missing board line in:\n%s", out)
	}

	parsed, err := markdown.Parse(out)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Parse: %v", err)
	}
	if parsed.Title != a.Title {
		t.Errorf("title: %q want %q", parsed.Title, a.Title)
	}
	if parsed.BoardName != "Welcome" {
		t.Errorf("board: %q want Welcome", parsed.BoardName)
	}
	if parsed.AuthorName != "alice" {
		t.Errorf("author: %q want alice", parsed.AuthorName)
	}
	if parsed.IDHint != 42 {
		t.Errorf("id: %d want 42", parsed.IDHint)
	}
	if parsed.ScoreHint != 5 {
		t.Errorf("score: %d want 5", parsed.ScoreHint)
	}
	if !parsed.CreatedAt.Equal(a.CreatedAt) {
		t.Errorf("created_at: %v want %v", parsed.CreatedAt, a.CreatedAt)
	}
	if !parsed.UpdatedAt.Equal(a.UpdatedAt.Time) {
		t.Errorf("updated_at: %v want %v", parsed.UpdatedAt, a.UpdatedAt.Time)
	}
	wantBody := "first line\nsecond line"
	if parsed.Body != wantBody {
		t.Errorf("body: %q want %q", parsed.Body, wantBody)
	}
	if len(parsed.Pushes) != 0 {
		t.Errorf("pushes: got %d, want 0", len(parsed.Pushes))
	}
}

func TestFormat_RoundTrip_WithPushes(t *testing.T) {
	a := sampleArticle()
	pushes := samplePushes()
	out, err := markdown.Format(a, pushes, markdown.FormatOpts{IncludePushes: true, BoardName: "Welcome"})
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Format: %v", err)
	}
	if !strings.Contains(out, markdown.PushesSentinel) {
		t.Fatalf("missing pushes sentinel in:\n%s", out)
	}

	parsed, err := markdown.Parse(out)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Parse: %v", err)
	}
	if len(parsed.Pushes) != 3 {
		t.Fatalf("pushes: got %d want 3 in:\n%s", len(parsed.Pushes), out)
	}
	wantKinds := []store.PushKind{store.PushKindPush, store.PushKindBoo, store.PushKindArrow}
	wantAuthors := []string{"bob", "carol", "dave"}
	wantBodies := []string{"great", "meh", "fyi just looking"}
	for i, p := range parsed.Pushes {
		if p.Kind != wantKinds[i] {
			t.Errorf("[%d] kind = %q want %q", i, p.Kind, wantKinds[i])
		}
		if p.Author != wantAuthors[i] {
			t.Errorf("[%d] author = %q want %q", i, p.Author, wantAuthors[i])
		}
		if p.Body != wantBodies[i] {
			t.Errorf("[%d] body = %q want %q", i, p.Body, wantBodies[i])
		}
		want := pushes[i].CreatedAt
		if !p.CreatedAt.Equal(want) {
			t.Errorf("[%d] created_at = %v want %v", i, p.CreatedAt, want)
		}
	}
}

func TestFormat_OmitPushesWhenIncludePushesFalse(t *testing.T) {
	a := sampleArticle()
	pushes := samplePushes()
	out, _ := markdown.Format(a, pushes, markdown.FormatOpts{IncludePushes: false, BoardName: "Welcome"})
	if strings.Contains(out, markdown.PushesSentinel) {
		t.Errorf("pushes sentinel emitted despite IncludePushes=false:\n%s", out)
	}
	parsed, _ := markdown.Parse(out)
	if len(parsed.Pushes) != 0 {
		t.Errorf("got %d pushes, want 0", len(parsed.Pushes))
	}
}

func TestParse_NoFrontmatter(t *testing.T) {
	in := "just a body\nwith two lines\n"
	parsed, err := markdown.Parse(in)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Parse: %v", err)
	}
	if parsed.Title != "" {
		t.Errorf("title: %q want empty", parsed.Title)
	}
	if parsed.Body != "just a body\nwith two lines" {
		t.Errorf("body: %q", parsed.Body)
	}
}

func TestParse_BodyContainingTripleDash(t *testing.T) {
	in := "---\ntitle: T\n---\n\nhead\n---\nmid\n---\ntail\n"
	parsed, err := markdown.Parse(in)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Parse: %v", err)
	}
	if parsed.Title != "T" {
		t.Errorf("title: %q", parsed.Title)
	}
	wantBody := "head\n---\nmid\n---\ntail"
	if parsed.Body != wantBody {
		t.Errorf("body:\n%q\nwant:\n%q", parsed.Body, wantBody)
	}
}

func TestParse_UnknownFrontmatterKey(t *testing.T) {
	in := "---\ntitle: T\nweird_field: weird_value\n---\n\nbody"
	parsed, _ := markdown.Parse(in)
	if parsed.Extra["weird_field"] != "weird_value" {
		t.Errorf("extra: %v", parsed.Extra)
	}
}

func TestParse_BodyContainingPushesSentinel(t *testing.T) {
	a := sampleArticle()
	a.Body = "Look at this:\n" + markdown.PushesSentinel + "\nthat is a literal in the body\n"
	out, err := markdown.Format(a, nil, markdown.FormatOpts{BoardName: "Welcome"})
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Format: %v", err)
	}
	parsed, err := markdown.Parse(out)
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("Parse: %v", err)
	}
	if !strings.Contains(parsed.Body, markdown.PushesSentinel) {
		t.Errorf("literal sentinel lost in round-trip:\n%s", parsed.Body)
	}
	if len(parsed.Pushes) != 0 {
		t.Errorf("body sentinel mistakenly parsed as pushes header: %d pushes", len(parsed.Pushes))
	}
}

func TestParse_PushesOnly_NoFrontmatter(t *testing.T) {
	in := markdown.PushesSentinel + "\n- 推 [alice] 2026-04-30T12:00:00Z  hi\n"
	parsed, _ := markdown.Parse(in)
	if parsed.Body != "" {
		t.Errorf("body should be empty, got %q", parsed.Body)
	}
	if len(parsed.Pushes) != 1 || parsed.Pushes[0].Body != "hi" {
		t.Errorf("pushes: %+v", parsed.Pushes)
	}
}

func TestParse_TitleWithColon(t *testing.T) {
	// Frontmatter parsing must split on the FIRST colon only.
	in := "---\ntitle: Welcome: A Tour\nboard: Welcome\n---\n\nbody"
	parsed, _ := markdown.Parse(in)
	if parsed.Title != "Welcome: A Tour" {
		t.Errorf("title: %q", parsed.Title)
	}
}

func TestFormat_NilArticle(t *testing.T) {
	if _, err := markdown.Format(nil, nil, markdown.FormatOpts{}); err == nil {
		t.Fatal("expected error for nil article")
	}
}

func TestParse_PushesWithSingleSpaceFallback(t *testing.T) {
	// Hand-edited file might not have the canonical 2-space separator.
	in := markdown.PushesSentinel + "\n- 推 [alice] 2026-04-30T12:00:00Z hi\n"
	parsed, _ := markdown.Parse(in)
	if len(parsed.Pushes) != 1 {
		t.Fatalf("pushes: %+v", parsed.Pushes)
	}
	got := parsed.Pushes[0]
	if got.Body != "hi" || got.Author != "alice" {
		t.Errorf("got %+v want body=hi author=alice", got)
	}
	want := mustParseTime(t, "2026-04-30T12:00:00Z")
	if !got.CreatedAt.Equal(want) {
		t.Errorf("ts: %v want %v", got.CreatedAt, want)
	}
}

1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Board struct {
11		ID          int64
12		Name        string
13		Title       string
14		Description string
15		BM          string
16		Attr        int64
17		CreatedAt   time.Time
18	}
19	
20	type BoardRepo struct{ s *Store }
21	
22	var ErrBoardNotFound = errors.New("board not found")
23	
24	const boardColumns = `id, name, title, description, bm, attr, created_at`
25	
26	func scanBoard(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Board, error) {
27		b := &Board{}
28		err := row.Scan(&b.ID, &b.Name, &b.Title, &b.Description, &b.BM, &b.Attr, &b.CreatedAt)
29		return b, err
30	}
31	
32	func (r *BoardRepo) List(ctx context.Context) ([]*Board, error) {
33		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
34			`SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards ORDER BY name COLLATE NOCASE`)
35		if err != nil {
36			return nil, err
37		}
38		defer rows.Close()
39		var out []*Board
40		for rows.Next() {
41			b, err := scanBoard(rows)
42			if err != nil {
43				return nil, err
44			}
45			out = append(out, b)
46		}
47		return out, rows.Err()
48	}
49	
50	func (r *BoardRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Board, error) {
51		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards WHERE id = ?`, id)
52		b, err := scanBoard(row)
53		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
54			return nil, ErrBoardNotFound
55		}
56		return b, err
57	}
58	
59	func (r *BoardRepo) GetByName(ctx context.Context, name string) (*Board, error) {
60		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards WHERE name = ? COLLATE NOCASE`, name)
61		b, err := scanBoard(row)
62		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
63			return nil, ErrBoardNotFound
64		}
65		return b, err
66	}
67	
68	// SeedDefaults is a belt-and-braces complement to migration 0002. It runs
69	// every startup; INSERT OR IGNORE makes it a no-op once the rows exist.
70	func (r *BoardRepo) SeedDefaults(ctx context.Context) error {
71		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
72		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
73		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `
74			INSERT OR IGNORE INTO boards (name, title, description) VALUES
75				('Welcome',  '歡迎來到 SSH-BBS', 'System welcome and announcements'),
76				('Test',     '測試板',           'Try things here. Posts may be cleared periodically.'),
77				('ChitChat', '閒聊',             'General chat. Be kind.')
78		`)
79		return err
80	}

1	// Package storetest provides shared helpers for tests that need a fresh
2	// SQLite store. It lives in its own package so any internal package can
3	// import it without creating cycles.
4	package storetest
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"testing"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
12	)
13	
14	// New opens a fresh on-disk SQLite store in t.TempDir(), runs migrations,
15	// and registers a Cleanup to close it. Use it whenever you'd write
16	// `store.Open` in a test — never share stores across tests.
17	func New(tb testing.TB) *store.Store {
18		tb.Helper()
19		dir := tb.TempDir()
20		st, err := store.Open(filepath.Join(dir, "test.db"))
21		if err != nil {
22			tb.Fatalf("store.Open: %v", err)
23		}
24		tb.Cleanup(func() { _ = st.Close() })
25		return st
26	}
27	
28	// MustUser inserts a user with a placeholder password hash and returns it.
29	// Convenient for tests that need a logged-in identity but don't care about auth.
30	func MustUser(tb testing.TB, st *store.Store, userID, nickname string) *store.User {
31		tb.Helper()
32		u, err := st.Users().Create(context.Background(), userID, "$2a$12$placeholderplaceholderplaceholderplaceholderplaceholder.", nickname, "")
33		if err != nil {
34			tb.Fatalf("create user %q: %v", userID, err)
35		}
36		return u
37	}
38	
39	// MustBoard returns a seeded board by name, failing the test if not found.
40	// Useful with the default Welcome / Test / ChitChat boards which
41	// SeedDefaults / migration 0002 create automatically.
42	func MustBoard(tb testing.TB, st *store.Store, name string) *store.Board {
43		tb.Helper()
44		b, err := st.Boards().GetByName(context.Background(), name)
45		if err != nil {
46			tb.Fatalf("get board %q: %v", name, err)
47		}
48		return b
49	}
50

1	# sshbbs
2	
3	A simplified PTT-style BBS served over SSH. Built with
4	[charmbracelet/wish](https://github.com/charmbracelet/wish) +
5	[bubbletea](https://github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea), backed by SQLite
6	(pure-Go via `modernc.org/sqlite`).
7	
8	![demo](docs/demo.gif)
9	
10	> Vim-style `h`/`j`/`k`/`l` navigation alongside arrow keys & Esc/Enter.
11	> The recording above shows login → boards → article → 推/噓 → `Ctrl+U`
12	> water-balloon inbox → disconnect, all driven from the keyboard.
13	> Re-record with `./scripts/record-demo.sh`.
14	
15	Implements the MUST-HAVE feature set of the planning doc:
16	
17	- Account register + login over SSH (bcrypt-hashed passwords)
18	- Default boards: `Welcome`, `Test`, `ChitChat`
19	- Read & post articles (UTF-8, CJK-aware widths via `go-runewidth`)
20	- 推文 (推 / 噓 / →) with live broadcast to other viewers of the same article
21	- 水球 (water balloons) — private one-line messages with offline persistence
22	  and on-reconnect replay
23	
24	## Quickstart
25	
26	```bash
27	make hostkey            # generates .ssh/host_ed25519 (run once)
28	make run                # starts the server on :2222
29	
30	# In another terminal, register:
31	ssh new@localhost -p 2222     # password is ignored — fill the in-TUI form
32	
33	# Then log in:
34	ssh alice@localhost -p 2222   # use the password you registered with
35	```
36	
37	## Local dev with Docker
38	
39	If you'd rather not install Go locally:
40	
41	```bash
42	make compose-up         # builds, generates a host key into a named volume, runs on :2222
43	ssh new@localhost -p 2222
44	make compose-down
45	```
46	
47	The `bbs-data` and `bbs-keys` volumes persist across restarts so accounts
48	and the host key survive `compose-down`/`compose-up`. See
49	[`docs/operations/04_docker.md`](docs/operations/04_docker.md) for what the
50	multi-stage build and `hostkey-init` service do, and a warning about
51	volume drivers that don't honour SQLite's filesystem locks (don't put
52	`bbs-data` on NFS).
53	
54	Inside the TUI:
55	
56	- `↑`/`↓` or `j`/`k` to move, `Enter` to select
57	- `p` in a board view to post
58	- `+` / `-` / `=` in an article view for 推 / 噓 / →
59	- `Ctrl+U` from anywhere logged-in to open the water-balloon inbox; `c` to
60	  compose, `r` to reply
61	- `Esc` / `Backspace` to navigate back
62	- `Ctrl+C` to disconnect
63	
64	## Two-user demo
65	
66	```bash
67	# T1
68	ssh alice@localhost -p 2222
69	
70	# T2
71	ssh bob@localhost -p 2222
72	
73	# From alice: Ctrl+U → c → bob → "hi" → Ctrl+S
74	# Bob sees a toast in the footer within ~100ms.
75	```
76	
77	## Common gotchas
78	
79	- **`Host key verification failed`** after running `make hostkey` again:
80	
81	  ```bash
82	  ssh-keygen -R '[localhost]:2222'
83	  ```
84	
85	- **CJK looks like garbage**: the client terminal must be UTF-8.
86	
87	  ```bash
88	  echo $LANG     # should match *.UTF-8
89	  ```
90	
91	- **`SQLITE_BUSY`** under high write load: the pure-Go driver is slower than
92	  the CGO `mattn/go-sqlite3`. WAL + a 5s busy_timeout + a process-level
93	  `writeMu` keeps us comfortable for dozens of users; swap drivers if it bites.
94	
95	## Layout
96	
97	```
98	cmd/sshbbs/             entrypoint, flag parsing, signal handling
99	internal/server/        wish bootstrap + password-auth middleware
100	internal/auth/          register/login (bcrypt, validation)
101	internal/store/         SQLite handle, migrations, repos
102	internal/store/migrations/  *.sql, embedded with go:embed
103	internal/chat/          in-memory broker (presence + live send)
104	internal/tui/           bubbletea root + per-screen models
105	docs/ptt_trace_code/    notes mapping pttbbs concepts to our schema
106	```
107	
108	## Development
109	
110	```bash
111	make build              # build the binary
112	make test               # run tests
113	make test-race          # race detector (CI standard)
114	make cover              # coverage by package
115	make db-reset           # delete the SQLite DB
116	make tidy               # go mod tidy
117	make fmt vet            # gofmt + go vet
118	```
119	
120	See `docs/testing.md` for the layered testing strategy.
121	
122	## Roadmap & lessons learned
123	
124	> See `docs/ptt_trace_code/00_overview.md` for what we deliberately don't
125	> implement, and the project plan
126	> `./.claude/plans/ptt-bbs-user-register-account-gentle-cookie.md` for the P1 / P2 backlog.
127	
128	Forward-looking work — long-term ideas, deferred items, things needing
129	evaluation — lives in [`TODO.md`](TODO.md), prioritised P1 → P3 with effort
130	estimates (S/M/L/XL). Items with accompanying research, design notes, or paused
131	troubleshooting link to a corresponding [`backlog/<slug>.md`](backlog/) doc.
132	
133	Backward-looking knowledge — past traps and non-obvious debugging — lives in
134	[`pitfalls/`](pitfalls/), titled by symptom so future-you can grep the error
135	message and land on the root cause + workaround instead of re-debugging from
136	scratch.
137	
138	## Resources
139	
140	- [ptt/pttbbs: PTT BBS source code](https://github.com/ptt/pttbbs)
141	  - [ptt/pttbbs | DeepWiki](https://deepwiki.com/ptt/pttbbs)
142	  - [Home · ptt/pttbbs Wiki](https://github.com/ptt/pttbbs/wiki)
143	- [批踢踢 - 維基百科，自由的百科全書](https://zh.wikipedia.org/zh-tw/%E6%89%B9%E8%B8%A2%E8%B8%A2)
144
```

```go
// Package seed loads default content (currently: welcome / intro articles)
// from embedded markdown files into the database on first startup.
//
// The contract is intentionally weak: a board with any existing article is
// skipped entirely. That way an admin who edits the seeded welcome article
// does not see their changes overwritten on the next restart.
//
// To add more seed articles, drop another *.md file under internal/seed/articles/
// with frontmatter naming the target board:
//
//	---
//	title: ...
//	board: Welcome
//	---
//
//	body...
//
// `go:embed` will pick it up at the next build.
package seed

import (
	"context"
	"embed"
	"errors"
	"fmt"
	"io/fs"
	"log"
	"sort"
	"strings"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/markdown"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
)

//go:embed articles/*.md
var articlesFS embed.FS

// Articles parses every embedded *.md file and inserts it into the board
// named by its frontmatter, attributing all of them to adminUserID. A
// board that already has at least one article is left alone, regardless
// of how many seed files target it.
//
// Failure to parse or insert any single file logs and continues — a bad
// seed file should not block startup. Missing boards are also skipped
// with a log line.
func Articles(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, adminUserID string) error {
	admin, err := st.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, adminUserID)
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("seed.Articles: lookup admin %q: %w", adminUserID, err)
	}

	entries, err := fs.ReadDir(articlesFS, "articles")
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("seed.Articles: read embedded fs: %w", err)
	}
	// Stable iteration order so logs are deterministic.
	sort.Slice(entries, func(i, j int) bool { return entries[i].Name() < entries[j].Name() })

	// Cache "has-articles?" per board so several seed files targeting the
	// same board only hit the DB once.
	boardEmpty := map[int64]bool{}

	for _, e := range entries {
		if e.IsDir() || !strings.HasSuffix(e.Name(), ".md") {
			continue
		}
		body, err := articlesFS.ReadFile("articles/" + e.Name())
		if err != nil {
			log.Printf("seed.Articles: read %s: %v", e.Name(), err)
			continue
		}
		parsed, err := markdown.Parse(string(body))
		if err != nil {
			log.Printf("seed.Articles: parse %s: %v", e.Name(), err)
			continue
		}
		if parsed.BoardName == "" {
			log.Printf("seed.Articles: %s has no `board:` frontmatter, skipping", e.Name())
			continue
		}
		if parsed.Title == "" {
			log.Printf("seed.Articles: %s has no `title:` frontmatter, skipping", e.Name())
			continue
		}

		board, err := st.Boards().GetByName(ctx, parsed.BoardName)
		if err != nil {
			if errors.Is(err, store.ErrBoardNotFound) {
				log.Printf("seed.Articles: %s targets board %q which doesn't exist; skipping",
					e.Name(), parsed.BoardName)
				continue
			}
			return fmt.Errorf("seed.Articles: lookup board %q: %w", parsed.BoardName, err)
		}

		empty, cached := boardEmpty[board.ID]
		if !cached {
			existing, err := st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, board.ID, 1)
			if err != nil {
				return fmt.Errorf("seed.Articles: list %q: %w", parsed.BoardName, err)
			}
			empty = len(existing) == 0
			boardEmpty[board.ID] = empty
		}
		if !empty {
			continue
		}

		if _, err := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, admin.ID, admin.UserID, parsed.Title, parsed.Body); err != nil {
			log.Printf("seed.Articles: insert %s: %v", e.Name(), err)
			continue
		}
		// Mark this board as no-longer-empty so subsequent seed files for
		// the same board are skipped.
		boardEmpty[board.ID] = false
		log.Printf("seed.Articles: seeded %s into board %q", e.Name(), parsed.BoardName)
	}
	return nil
}

```

```md
---
title: 歡迎來到 SSH-BBS
board: Welcome
author: admin
---

哈囉，歡迎來到這個簡化版的 PTT 風格 BBS。整個站台就是一個 SSH server：
你看到的所有東西都跑在 wish + bubbletea 上面，後端用 SQLite 存資料。

# 這裡能做什麼

- **看板**：預設有 `Welcome`（這裡）、`Test`、`ChitChat`。
- **發文 / 讀文**：UTF-8 全程支援，CJK 寬度自動處理。
- **推文**：在文章畫面按 `+` 推、`-` 噓、`=` 留 →。其他線上看同篇的人會即時看到。
- **水球**：私訊一行話。`Ctrl+U` 開水球收件匣，離線收到的也會在你重連時補播。
- **站內信 (mail)**：水球留不下，重要的話寫信。`Ctrl+U` 進收件匣後切到郵件分頁。

# 鍵盤總覽

\```
↑/↓ 或 j/k     上下移動
←/→ 或 h/l     上下層導覽（表單例外，h/l 留給文字編輯）
Enter / Space  進入 / 翻頁
Esc / Backspace 返回
g / G          捲到頂 / 底
[ / ]          上一篇 / 下一篇
p              在看板裡發新文
+ / - / =      推 / 噓 / →
y              匯出此篇為 markdown
E              編輯此篇（作者本人或 mod 以上）
D              刪除此篇 / 推文（作者本人或 mod 以上）
Ctrl+U         水球 / 站內信
Ctrl+C         斷線離開
\```

# 權限

- **guest**：唯讀，只看不寫。SSH 用 `guest` 帳號連進來不需密碼。
- **user**：一般註冊帳號。`ssh new@host -p 2222` 註冊，密碼欄位會被忽略，用 TUI 內表單填。
- **mod**：可以刪 / 編輯任何人的文與推文。
- **admin**：站長，可以調整他人權限。

# 文章是 markdown

每篇文章都能用 markdown 表達。在文章畫面按 `y` 會跳到匯出檢視，
你可以選 (1) 純內文 (2) 含留言 (3) 寫到伺服器端檔案 (`data/exports/<你的帳號>/`)，
或按 `c` 推到終端機剪貼簿（OSC 52，需終端機支援）。

寫文也可以走 markdown 路：在發文畫面按 `Ctrl+I`，貼上含 frontmatter 的 markdown，
標題會自動從 `title:` 帶進來。

# 這篇文是怎麼長出來的

這篇 welcome 不是寫死在程式碼或 DB migration 裡。它是 `internal/seed/articles/welcome.md`，
用 `go:embed` 包進 binary，server 第一次發現 `Welcome` 看板是空的就把它寫進 DB。
之後 admin 改過一次，下次重啟也不會被覆蓋（種子的冪等規則：看板有任何文就跳過）。

要新增種子文章，丟一個新的 `*.md` 進那個資料夾，frontmatter 寫 `board: 看板名` 就好。

# 想了解更多

- 專案 README：repo 根目錄 `README.md`
- pttbbs 對照筆記：`docs/ptt_trace_code/*`
- 測試策略：`docs/testing.md`
- 待辦清單：`TODO.md`（含 P1～P3 + 工作量估計）

package seed_test

import (
	"context"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/seed"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store/storetest"
)

// mustAdmin inserts an admin-role system user and returns its UserID
// (string) so seed.Articles can look it up the same way main.go does.
func mustAdmin(t *testing.T, st *store.Store) string {
	t.Helper()
	if _, err := st.Users().InsertSystemAccount(
		context.Background(), "admin", "$2a$12$placeholderplaceholderplaceholderplaceholderplaceholder.",
		store.RoleAdmin, false,
	); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("seed admin: %v", err)
	}
	return "admin"
}

func TestArticles_SeedsWhenBoardEmpty(t *testing.T) {
	ctx := context.Background()
	st := storetest.New(t)
	if err := st.Boards().SeedDefaults(ctx); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("SeedDefaults: %v", err)
	}
	admin := mustAdmin(t, st)

	if err := seed.Articles(ctx, st, admin); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("seed.Articles: %v", err)
	}

	welcome, err := st.Boards().GetByName(ctx, "Welcome")
	if err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("GetByName: %v", err)
	}
	got, _ := st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, welcome.ID, 10)
	if len(got) != 1 {
		t.Fatalf("Welcome article count = %d, want 1", len(got))
	}
	if got[0].AuthorUserID != "admin" {
		t.Errorf("author = %q, want admin", got[0].AuthorUserID)
	}
	if !strings.Contains(got[0].Title, "歡迎") {
		t.Errorf("title = %q, want it to contain 歡迎", got[0].Title)
	}
	if got[0].Body == "" {
		t.Error("body is empty")
	}
}

func TestArticles_NoOpWhenBoardHasContent(t *testing.T) {
	ctx := context.Background()
	st := storetest.New(t)
	if err := st.Boards().SeedDefaults(ctx); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("SeedDefaults: %v", err)
	}
	admin := mustAdmin(t, st)

	welcome, _ := st.Boards().GetByName(ctx, "Welcome")
	// Pre-seed a fake article so seed.Articles should skip Welcome.
	if _, err := st.Articles().Create(ctx, welcome.ID, 1, "admin", "user-edited", "user-body"); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("seed pre-existing: %v", err)
	}

	if err := seed.Articles(ctx, st, admin); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("seed.Articles: %v", err)
	}

	got, _ := st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, welcome.ID, 10)
	if len(got) != 1 {
		t.Fatalf("Welcome article count = %d, want 1 (the pre-existing)", len(got))
	}
	if got[0].Title != "user-edited" {
		t.Errorf("title = %q, want 'user-edited' (pre-existing)", got[0].Title)
	}
}

func TestArticles_IdempotentOverRestarts(t *testing.T) {
	ctx := context.Background()
	st := storetest.New(t)
	if err := st.Boards().SeedDefaults(ctx); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("SeedDefaults: %v", err)
	}
	admin := mustAdmin(t, st)

	for i := 0; i < 3; i++ {
		if err := seed.Articles(ctx, st, admin); err != nil {
			t.Fatalf("seed run #%d: %v", i, err)
		}
	}

	welcome, _ := st.Boards().GetByName(ctx, "Welcome")
	got, _ := st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, welcome.ID, 10)
	if len(got) != 1 {
		t.Errorf("after 3 seed runs: %d articles, want 1", len(got))
	}
}

func TestArticles_AdminMissing(t *testing.T) {
	ctx := context.Background()
	st := storetest.New(t)
	if err := st.Boards().SeedDefaults(ctx); err != nil {
		t.Fatalf("SeedDefaults: %v", err)
	}
	if err := seed.Articles(ctx, st, "admin"); err == nil {
		t.Error("expected error when admin user does not exist")
	}
}
